print("📁 Remaining directories under data/:")
for r in sorted(remaining):
    print(" -", r)


# [CELL] 
# Cell 1 — Define raw vs processed extensions
import shutil
from pathlib import Path

DATA_DIR = Path("data")
RAW_DIR = DATA_DIR / "raw"
PROCESSED_DIR = DATA_DIR / "processed"
RAW_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
PROCESSED_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

# Rough heuristics: tweak if needed
RAW_EXTS = {
    "fa", "fasta", "gff3", "gtf", "gz", "tar", "tar_gz", "zip", "rds", "rdata",
    "sql", "wb", "wib", "hal", "ace", "bw", "vcf", "2bit"
}
PROCESSED_EXTS = {
    "csv", "tsv", "json", "yaml", "yml", "xml", "npz", "npy", "pkl", "pt", 
    "xlsx", "xls", "parquet", "txt", "log"
}

# collect all current type folders in data/
type_folders = [d for d in DATA_DIR.iterdir() if d.is_dir() and d.name not in {"raw", "processed", "Originals"}]
print(f"Found {len(type_folders)} type-based folders to classify.")


# [CELL] 
# Cell 2 — Build move plan
move_plan = []
for folder in type_folders:
    name = folder.name.lower()
    if name in RAW_EXTS:
        target = RAW_DIR / name
    elif name in PROCESSED_EXTS:
        target = PROCESSED_DIR / name
    else:
        # default: unknown goes to processed (safer)
        target = PROCESSED_DIR / name
    move_plan.append((folder, target))

print("📂 Planned classification:")
for src, dst in move_plan:
    print(f" - {src.name} → {dst}")


# [CELL] 
# Cell 3 — Execute move
DRY_RUN = False  # change to False to actually move folders

if DRY_RUN:
    print("ℹ️ DRY_RUN=True — preview only. Set to False to execute.")
else:
    moved = 0
    for src, dst in move_plan:
        if not dst.exists():
            dst.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        try:
            shutil.move(str(src), str(dst))
            moved += 1
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"⚠️ Could not move {src}: {e}")
    print(f"✅ Moved {moved} folders.")


# [CELL] 
# Cell 4 — Show counts in each new category
import os

def summarize_folder(base):
    summary = []
    for sub in sorted(base.iterdir()):
        if sub.is_dir():
            total_size = sum(
                os.path.getsize(os.path.join(root, f))
                for root, _, files in os.walk(sub)
                for f in files
            )
            file_count = sum(len(files) for _, _, files in os.walk(sub))
            summary.append({
                "folder": sub.name,
                "file_count": file_count,
                "size_MB": round(total_size / (1024 * 1024), 2)
            })
    return pd.DataFrame(summary).sort_values("size_MB", ascending=False)

print("📊 Raw folders:")
display(summarize_folder(RAW_DIR))
print("📊 Processed folders:")
display(summarize_folder(PROCESSED_DIR))

# [CELL] 
# Cell 1 — Setup & load data
import os
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

ROOT = Path.cwd()
DATA_RAW = ROOT / "data" / "raw"
DATA_PROCESSED = ROOT / "data" / "processed"
EXPERIMENTS_OUT = DATA_PROCESSED / "experiments"
EXPERIMENTS_OUT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# Load expression (neurons x genes, columns are WBGene IDs)
expr_path = DATA_PROCESSED / "csv" / "gene_expr_CeNGEN_base_mapped.csv"
expr_df = pd.read_csv(expr_path, index_col=0)

# Load connectome edges (Source, Target, SynapseCount)
edges_path = DATA_PROCESSED / "csv" / "merged_edges_with_relatedness.csv"
edges_df = pd.read_csv(edges_path)
edges_df.columns = [c.lower() for c in edges_df.columns]

src_col = [c for c in edges_df.columns if "source" in c][0]
tgt_col = [c for c in edges_df.columns if "target" in c][0]
w_col   = "synapsecount" if "synapsecount" in edges_df.columns else None

print(f"expr_df: {expr_df.shape}; edges: {edges_df.shape}; weight_col: {w_col}")


# [CELL] 
# Cell 2 — Graph, features, and targets
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# Keep only neurons present in expression
neurons = expr_df.index.tolist()
neuron_to_idx = {n:i for i,n in enumerate(neurons)}
N = len(neurons)

# Build (directed) adjacency list from edges; drop edges where nodes not in expr_df
mask_src = edges_df[src_col].isin(neurons)
mask_tgt = edges_df[tgt_col].isin(neurons)
Edf = edges_df[mask_src & mask_tgt].copy()

# Edge weights
if w_col is None:
    Edf["w"] = 1.0
else:
    Edf["w"] = Edf[w_col].astype(float).clip(lower=0.0)

# Sparse adjacency in COO form
rows = Edf[src_col].map(neuron_to_idx).values
cols = Edf[tgt_col].map(neuron_to_idx).values
wts  = Edf["w"].values.astype(np.float32)

# Target: mean outgoing synaptic strength per neuron (normalize)
out_w = Edf.groupby(src_col)["w"].mean().reindex(neurons).fillna(0.0).values.astype(np.float32)
y = (out_w - out_w.mean()) / (out_w.std() + 1e-6)  # z-score target

# ==== Features ====
USE_PCA = True        # set False to use raw subset of genes
N_PCA   = 32          # PCA dims if USE_PCA=True
RAW_K   = 512         # top-variance genes if USE_PCA=False

if USE_PCA:
    scaler = StandardScaler(with_mean=True, with_std=True)
    X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(expr_df.values)         # (N x G)
    pca = PCA(n_components=min(N_PCA, X_scaled.shape[1]), random_state=42)
    X_feat = pca.fit_transform(X_scaled).astype(np.float32) # (N x d)
else:
    # raw features: top-variance genes
    top_genes = expr_df.var(axis=0).sort_values(ascending=False).head(RAW_K).index
    X_feat = expr_df.loc[:, top_genes].fillna(0.0).values.astype(np.float32)
    # z-score per feature
    X_feat = (X_feat - X_feat.mean(0)) / (X_feat.std(0) + 1e-6)

print(f"N={N}, edges kept={len(rows)}, features={X_feat.shape}, target y shape={y.shape}")


# [CELL] 
# Cell 3 — Graph Neural Cellular Automata (GNCA)
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print("device:", device)

# Convert arrays to torch
X_feat_t = torch.from_numpy(X_feat).to(device)              # (N, d_in)
y_t       = torch.from_numpy(y).to(device)                  # (N,)

# Build sparse adjacency (row-normalized for message passing)
# We'll use outgoing adjacency (source -> target)
indices = torch.stack([
    torch.from_numpy(rows).long(),
    torch.from_numpy(cols).long()
], dim=0).to(device)
values  = torch.from_numpy(wts).to(device)
A = torch.sparse_coo_tensor(indices, values, size=(N, N)).coalesce()

# Row-normalize A
row_sum = torch.sparse.sum(A, dim=1).to_dense().clamp_min(1e-6)  # (N,)
row_inv = 1.0 / row_sum
row_inv_diag = torch.sparse_coo_tensor(
    torch.stack([torch.arange(N), torch.arange(N)]).to(device),
    row_inv, size=(N, N)
).coalesce()
A_norm = torch.sparse.mm(row_inv_diag, A).coalesce()  # normalized by out-degree weight sum

class GNCA(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_dim, state_dim=64, fire_rate=0.5):
        super().__init__()
        self.state_dim  = state_dim
        self.fire_rate  = fire_rate
        # init embed from features to state
        self.inp = nn.Linear(in_dim, state_dim)
        # message and update (residual)
        self.msg = nn.Linear(state_dim, state_dim, bias=False)
        self.upd = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(2*state_dim, 128),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Linear(128, state_dim)
        )
        # readout head to scalar
        self.readout = nn.Sequential(
            nn.LayerNorm(state_dim),
            nn.Linear(state_dim, 1)
        )

    def forward(self, x_feat, A_norm, steps=8, train_mode=True):
        # initialize state from features
        x = torch.tanh(self.inp(x_feat))   # (N, C)
        for t in range(steps):
            # messages from neighbors: A_norm @ msg(x)
            m = torch.sparse.mm(A_norm, self.msg(x))
            # concat self-state and neighbor-message
            z = torch.cat([x, m], dim=-1)
            dx = self.upd(z)
            # stochastic "fire" mask for asynch updates
            if train_mode and self.fire_rate < 1.0:
                mask = (torch.rand_like(dx[:, :1]) < self.fire_rate).float()
                dx = dx * mask
            # residual update
            x = x + dx
        # readout scalar per node
        yhat = self.readout(x).squeeze(-1)
        return yhat, x

model = GNCA(in_dim=X_feat_t.shape[1], state_dim=64, fire_rate=0.5).to(device)
sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())/1e6


# [CELL] 
# Cell 4 — Train GNCA to predict outgoing synaptic strength
import math
torch.manual_seed(42)

optim = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, weight_decay=1e-4)
sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optim, T_max=200, eta_min=3e-4)
loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()

EPOCHS = 200
STEPS  = 8  # unroll steps

best = math.inf
for ep in range(1, EPOCHS+1):
    model.train()
    yhat, _ = model(X_feat_t, A_norm, steps=STEPS, train_mode=True)
    loss = loss_fn(yhat, y_t)
    optim.zero_grad()
    loss.backward()
    nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
    optim.step()
    sched.step()

    if loss.item() < best:
        best = loss.item()
        torch.save(model.state_dict(), EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_best.pt")

    if ep % 20 == 0 or ep == 1:
        with torch.no_grad():
            # Pearson r
            ycpu = y_t.detach().cpu().numpy()
            phat = yhat.detach().cpu().numpy()
            r = np.corrcoef(ycpu, phat)[0,1]
        print(f"Epoch {ep:03d} | loss={loss.item():.4f} | r={r:.3f}")

print("Best loss:", best)


# [CELL] 
# Cell 5 — Final eval & export
model.load_state_dict(torch.load(EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_best.pt", map_location=device))
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    yhat, X_final = model(X_feat_t, A_norm, steps=8, train_mode=False)
    yhat = yhat.detach().cpu().numpy()
    ytrue = y_t.detach().cpu().numpy()
    r = np.corrcoef(ytrue, yhat)[0,1]
    mae = np.abs(ytrue - yhat).mean()
print(f"Final Pearson r={r:.3f} | MAE={mae:.3f}")

pred_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "neuron": neurons,
    "y_true_z": ytrue,
    "y_pred_z": yhat
})
pred_path = EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_pred_outgoing_strength.csv"
pred_df.to_csv(pred_path, index=False)
print("Saved:", pred_path)


# [CELL] 
# Cell A — Shuffle control: permute expression rows; structure should vanish (r ~ 0)
import torch, numpy as np
torch.manual_seed(123)

perm = torch.randperm(X_feat_t.shape[0], device=X_feat_t.device)
X_feat_shuf = X_feat_t[perm]  # break neuron-expression alignment

model_shuf = GNCA(in_dim=X_feat_t.shape[1], state_dim=64, fire_rate=0.5).to(device)
opt = torch.optim.AdamW(model_shuf.parameters(), lr=3e-3, weight_decay=1e-4)
sch = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=80, eta_min=5e-4)
loss_fn = torch.nn.MSELoss()

EPOCHS = 80
for ep in range(1, EPOCHS+1):
    model_shuf.train()
    yhat, _ = model_shuf(X_feat_shuf, A_norm, steps=8, train_mode=True)
    loss = loss_fn(yhat, y_t)
    opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model_shuf.parameters(), 1.0); opt.step(); sch.step()
    if ep % 20 == 0 or ep == 1:
        with torch.no_grad():
            r = np.corrcoef(y_t.detach().cpu().numpy(), yhat.detach().cpu().numpy())[0,1]
        print(f"[SHUF] Epoch {ep:03d} | loss={loss.item():.4f} | r={r:.3f}")


# [CELL] 
# Cell B — Edge dropout (50%) and small latent (state_dim=8)
import torch

drop_rate = 0.5
vals = A_norm.values()
mask = (torch.rand_like(vals) > drop_rate).float()
A_drop = torch.sparse_coo_tensor(A_norm.indices(), vals * mask, A_norm.size()).coalesce()

model_small = GNCA(in_dim=X_feat_t.shape[1], state_dim=8, fire_rate=0.5).to(device)
opt = torch.optim.AdamW(model_small.parameters(), lr=3e-3, weight_decay=1e-4)
sch = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=120, eta_min=5e-4)
loss_fn = torch.nn.MSELoss()

EPOCHS = 120
for ep in range(1, EPOCHS+1):
    model_small.train()
    yhat, _ = model_small(X_feat_t, A_drop, steps=8, train_mode=True)
    loss = loss_fn(yhat, y_t)
    opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model_small.parameters(), 1.0); opt.step(); sch.step()
    if ep % 20 == 0 or ep == 1:
        with torch.no_grad():
            r = np.corrcoef(y_t.detach().cpu().numpy(), yhat.detach().cpu().numpy())[0,1]
        print(f"[DROP{int(drop_rate*100)}%|d=8] Epoch {ep:03d} | loss={loss.item():.4f} | r={r:.3f}")


# [CELL] 
# Cell C — Saliency and Integrated Gradients (IG) for feature importance
import torch, numpy as np, pandas as pd

# Use your best trained model 'model' and normalized adj A_norm
model.eval()

def predict_from_input(x, steps=8):
    yhat, _ = model(x, A_norm, steps=steps, train_mode=False)
    return yhat

# --- Saliency (|∂y/∂x| aggregated across neurons) ---
X_feat_t.requires_grad_(True)
yhat = predict_from_input(X_feat_t, steps=8)
# sum over neurons to get scalar objective (or use MSE to true y for task-specific grads)
obj = yhat.sum()
model.zero_grad(); 
if X_feat_t.grad is not None: X_feat_t.grad.zero_()
obj.backward(retain_graph=True)

sal = X_feat_t.grad.detach().abs().mean(dim=0).cpu().numpy()  # feature-wise mean |grad|
sal_df = pd.DataFrame({"feature_idx": np.arange(len(sal)), "saliency": sal}).sort_values("saliency", ascending=False)
sal_path = EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_saliency_features.csv"
sal_df.to_csv(sal_path, index=False)
print("Saved saliency →", sal_path)
X_feat_t.requires_grad_(False)

# --- Integrated Gradients (baseline = zeros) ---
def integrated_gradients(x, steps=32):
    baseline = torch.zeros_like(x)
    grads_accum = torch.zeros_like(x)
    for a in torch.linspace(0, 1, steps, device=x.device):
        x_a = baseline + a * (x - baseline)
        x_a.requires_grad_(True)
        y_a = predict_from_input(x_a, steps=8).sum()
        model.zero_grad()
        if x_a.grad is not None: x_a.grad.zero_()
        y_a.backward()
        grads_accum += x_a.grad
    ig = (x - baseline) * (grads_accum / steps)
    return ig

with torch.no_grad():
    pass  # ensure no leftover grads

ig = integrated_gradients(X_feat_t, steps=32)
ig_feat = ig.detach().cpu().numpy().mean(axis=0)  # mean over neurons
ig_df = pd.DataFrame({"feature_idx": np.arange(len(ig_feat)), "IG": ig_feat}).sort_values("IG", ascending=False)
ig_path = EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_integrated_gradients_features.csv"
ig_df.to_csv(ig_path, index=False)
print("Saved IG →", ig_path)

# Quick peek at top features
print("Top saliency features:", sal_df.head(10).to_dict(orient="records"))
print("Top IG features:", ig_df.head(10).to_dict(orient="records"))


# [CELL] 
# Cell D — Compare graph-only vs expression-only predictive power
import torch, numpy as np
from torch import nn
from copy import deepcopy

def train_gnca_baseline(X_in, A_in, label, name, steps=8, state_dim=64, epochs=100):
    """Train a small GNCA on given inputs and adjacency, return final r."""
    model_b = GNCA(in_dim=X_in.shape[1], state_dim=state_dim, fire_rate=0.5).to(device)
    opt = torch.optim.AdamW(model_b.parameters(), lr=3e-3, weight_decay=1e-4)
    sch = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=epochs, eta_min=5e-4)
    loss_fn = nn.MSELoss()
    best = 1e9; best_r = 0
    for ep in range(1, epochs+1):
        model_b.train()
        yhat, _ = model_b(X_in, A_in, steps=steps, train_mode=True)
        loss = loss_fn(yhat, label)
        opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model_b.parameters(),1.0); opt.step(); sch.step()
        if loss.item() < best: best = loss.item()
        if ep % 20 == 0 or ep == 1:
            with torch.no_grad():
                r = np.corrcoef(label.detach().cpu().numpy(), yhat.detach().cpu().numpy())[0,1]
            print(f"[{name}] Epoch {ep:03d} | loss={loss.item():.4f} | r={r:.3f}")
            best_r = r
    return best_r

# ---- Graph-only baseline ----
# Replace expression with constant vector (no transcript info)
X_const = torch.ones_like(X_feat_t)  # all ones

print("\n=== Graph-only baseline ===")
r_graph = train_gnca_baseline(X_const, A_norm, y_t, "GRAPH-ONLY")

# ---- Expression-only baseline ----
# Replace adjacency with fully connected (uniform weights)
N = X_feat_t.shape[0]
dense_vals = torch.ones(N*N, device=device)
dense_idx  = torch.stack([torch.arange(N).repeat_interleave(N),
                          torch.arange(N).repeat(N)]).to(device)
A_dense = torch.sparse_coo_tensor(dense_idx, dense_vals, size=(N,N)).coalesce()
# Normalize
row_sum = torch.sparse.sum(A_dense, dim=1).to_dense().clamp_min(1e-6)
row_inv = 1.0 / row_sum
diag_idx = torch.stack([torch.arange(N), torch.arange(N)]).to(device)
A_dense_norm = torch.sparse_coo_tensor(diag_idx, row_inv, (N,N)).coalesce()
A_dense_norm = torch.sparse.mm(A_dense_norm, A_dense).coalesce()

print("\n=== Expression-only baseline ===")
r_expr = train_gnca_baseline(X_feat_t, A_dense_norm, y_t, "EXPR-ONLY")

# ---- Comparison summary ----
print("\n\n=== Baseline Comparison ===")
print(f"Graph-only r = {r_graph:.3f}")
print(f"Expression-only r = {r_expr:.3f}")


# [CELL] 
# Cell D-prep — Rebuild motif_matrix.csv from connectome
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, networkx as nx
from pathlib import Path
from tqdm import tqdm

DATA_ROOT = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data")
edges_path = DATA_ROOT / "processed" / "csv" / "merged_edges_with_relatedness.csv"
motif_out = DATA_ROOT / "processed" / "motifs" / "motif_matrix.csv"
motif_out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# === 1️⃣ Load connectome edges ===
edges = pd.read_csv(edges_path)
print("Loaded edges:", edges.shape)
print("Columns:", edges.columns.tolist())

# Expect: ['Source', 'Target', 'Type', 'SynapseCount', 'SourceFile', 'Relatedness']
G = nx.DiGraph()
for _, r in edges.iterrows():
    G.add_edge(r["Source"], r["Target"], weight=r.get("SynapseCount", 1.0))

print(f"Graph built: {G.number_of_nodes()} neurons, {G.number_of_edges()} synapses")

# === 2️⃣ Compute motif participation ===
# We'll detect: 0=reciprocal, 1=chain (A→B→C), 2=feedforward loop (A→B, A→C, B→C),
# 3=divergent (A→B, A→C), 4=convergent (A→C, B→C)
motif_names = ["reciprocal", "chain", "feedforward", "divergent", "convergent"]
motif_participation = {n: np.zeros(len(motif_names), dtype=int) for n in G.nodes()}

nodes = list(G.nodes())
for i, a in enumerate(tqdm(nodes, desc="Scanning motifs")):
    for b in G.successors(a):
        # reciprocal
        if G.has_edge(b, a):
            motif_participation[a][0] = 1
            motif_participation[b][0] = 1
        # divergent (A→B, A→C)
        for c in G.successors(a):
            if b != c:
                motif_participation[a][3] = 1
                motif_participation[b][4] = 1  # B as convergent input target
                motif_participation[c][4] = 1
                # feedforward (A→B, A→C, B→C)
                if G.has_edge(b, c):
                    motif_participation[a][2] = 1
                    motif_participation[b][1] = 1  # part of chain
                    motif_participation[c][4] = 1
        # chain (A→B→C)
        for c in G.successors(b):
            if c != a:
                motif_participation[a][1] = 1
                motif_participation[b][1] = 1
                motif_participation[c][1] = 1

# === 3️⃣ Save neuron × motif matrix ===
motif_df = pd.DataFrame.from_dict(motif_participation, orient="index", columns=motif_names)
motif_df.index.name = "Neuron"
motif_df.to_csv(motif_out)
print(f"✅ Saved motif matrix → {motif_out}")
print(motif_df.sum().to_string())
print("Matrix shape:", motif_df.shape)


# [CELL] 
# Verify & align motif matrix with expression dataset
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

motif_path = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data/processed/motifs/motif_matrix.csv")
motif_df = pd.read_csv(motif_path, index_col=0)

print(f"Motif matrix loaded: {motif_df.shape}")
print(f"Expression matrix: {expr_df.shape}")

common_neurons = sorted(set(expr_df.index) & set(motif_df.index))
motif_df = motif_df.loc[common_neurons]
expr_df = expr_df.loc[common_neurons]

print(f"Aligned neurons: {len(common_neurons)}")
motif_df.to_csv(motif_path)  # overwrite aligned version
print("✅ Motif matrix aligned and saved.")


# [CELL] 
# Fix neuron name mismatch (trim whitespace, unify case, align)
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

motif_path = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data/processed/motifs/motif_matrix.csv")
motif_df = pd.read_csv(motif_path, index_col=0)
print("Original motif_df shape:", motif_df.shape)

# Clean neuron labels (strip spaces, unify uppercase)
motif_df.index = motif_df.index.astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()
expr_df.index = expr_df.index.astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()

# Find intersection
common_neurons = sorted(set(expr_df.index) & set(motif_df.index))
print(f"Found {len(common_neurons)} overlapping neurons out of {len(expr_df)} expr entries")

# Subset both
motif_df = motif_df.loc[common_neurons]
expr_df = expr_df.loc[common_neurons]

# Sanity check
print("Aligned motif_df shape:", motif_df.shape)
print("Aligned expr_df shape:", expr_df.shape)

# [CELL] 
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

motif_path = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data/processed/motifs/motif_matrix.csv")

# 🩹 Read flexibly: try comma first, then tab
try:
    motif_df = pd.read_csv(motif_path, index_col=0)
except Exception:
    motif_df = pd.read_csv(motif_path, sep="\t", index_col=0)

print("Raw motif_df shape:", motif_df.shape)
print("Raw columns:", motif_df.columns.tolist()[:10])

# 🧽 Fix accidental merged header (if only 1 giant column name detected)
if len(motif_df.columns) == 1 and "," in motif_df.columns[0]:
    motif_df.columns = [c.strip() for c in motif_df.columns[0].split(",")]
    motif_df = motif_df.apply(lambda x: x.astype(str).str.split(",")).applymap(lambda x: float(x[0]) if isinstance(x, list) else x)

elif len(motif_df.columns) == 1 and "\t" in motif_df.columns[0]:
    motif_df.columns = [c.strip() for c in motif_df.columns[0].split("\t")]
    motif_df = motif_df.apply(lambda x: x.astype(str).str.split("\t")).applymap(lambda x: float(x[0]) if isinstance(x, list) else x)

# 🧩 Clean neuron names (trim and uppercase)
motif_df.index = motif_df.index.astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()
expr_df.index = expr_df.index.astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()

# ✅ Align
common_neurons = sorted(set(expr_df.index) & set(motif_df.index))
motif_df = motif_df.loc[common_neurons]
expr_df = expr_df.loc[common_neurons]

print(f"✅ Aligned motif_df: {motif_df.shape}, expr_df: {expr_df.shape}")
motif_df.head()


# [CELL] 
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from tqdm import tqdm

# Paths
edges_path = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data/processed/csv/merged_edges_with_relatedness.csv")
motif_path = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data/processed/motifs/motif_matrix.csv")

# Load and clean
edges = pd.read_csv(edges_path)
edges["Source"] = edges["Source"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()
edges["Target"] = edges["Target"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()

neurons = sorted(set(edges["Source"]) | set(edges["Target"]))
motif_df = pd.DataFrame(0, index=neurons, columns=["reciprocal", "chain", "feedforward", "divergent", "convergent"])

print(f"Building motif matrix for {len(neurons)} neurons and {len(edges)} edges…")

# --- Divergent / Convergent / Reciprocal ---
for _, row in tqdm(edges.iterrows(), total=len(edges), desc="Stage 1: Divergent+Convergent+Reciprocal"):
    src, tgt = row["Source"], row["Target"]
    motif_df.loc[src, "divergent"] += 1
    motif_df.loc[tgt, "convergent"] += 1
    if (edges["Source"].eq(tgt) & edges["Target"].eq(src)).any():
        motif_df.loc[src, "reciprocal"] += 1
        motif_df.loc[tgt, "reciprocal"] += 1

# --- Chain and Feed-forward ---
for n in tqdm(neurons, desc="Stage 2: Chain+Feedforward"):
    downstream = edges.loc[edges["Source"] == n, "Target"].tolist()
    for t in downstream:
        second_hop = edges.loc[edges["Source"] == t, "Target"].tolist()
        for u in second_hop:
            if n != u:
                motif_df.loc[n, "chain"] += 1
                motif_df.loc[u, "feedforward"] += 1

# Normalize
motif_df = (motif_df > 0).astype(int)

# Save
motif_df.to_csv(motif_path)
print(f"\n✅ Rebuilt motif matrix → {motif_path}")
print(f"Shape: {motif_df.shape}")
print("Motif counts:")
print(motif_df.sum())


# [CELL] 
# Align expression and motif matrices
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

motif_path = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data/processed/motifs/motif_matrix.csv")
motif_df = pd.read_csv(motif_path, index_col=0)

# Normalize neuron IDs
motif_df.index = motif_df.index.astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()
expr_df.index = expr_df.index.astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()

# Align intersection
common_neurons = sorted(set(expr_df.index) & set(motif_df.index))
expr_df = expr_df.loc[common_neurons]
motif_df = motif_df.loc[common_neurons]

print(f"✅ Aligned expression: {expr_df.shape}, motif: {motif_df.shape}")


# [CELL] 
# ============================================================
# 🧠 Cell E — Graph-Neural Cellular Automaton (GNCA)
# Predicts motif participation (reciprocal, chain, etc.) from expression + connectome
# ============================================================

import torch, torch.nn as nn, torch.nn.functional as F
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import numpy as np, pandas as pd
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 🔧 Patch: Remove duplicate neuron indices
# ------------------------------------------------------------
def dedup_index(df, label):
    before = len(df)
    df = df[~df.index.duplicated(keep="first")]
    after = len(df)
    if before != after:
        print(f"⚠️  Removed {before - after} duplicate indices from {label}")
    return df

expr_df = dedup_index(expr_df, "expr_df")
motif_df = dedup_index(motif_df, "motif_df")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1️⃣  Sanitize and align neuron indices
# ------------------------------------------------------------
expr_df.index  = expr_df.index.astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()
motif_df.index = motif_df.index.astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()
edges_df["Source"] = edges_df["Source"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()
edges_df["Target"] = edges_df["Target"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()

common_neurons = sorted(set(expr_df.index) & set(motif_df.index))
print(f"✅ Common neurons found: {len(common_neurons)}")

# Safe reindexing (drop NaNs automatically)
expr_df  = expr_df.reindex(common_neurons).dropna()
motif_df = motif_df.reindex(common_neurons).dropna()

print(f"✅ After alignment: expr={expr_df.shape}, motif={motif_df.shape}")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2️⃣  Build adjacency matrix (undirected)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
neuron_index = {n: i for i, n in enumerate(common_neurons)}
N = len(common_neurons)
A = torch.zeros((N, N), dtype=torch.float32)

for _, row in edges_df.iterrows():
    s, t = row["Source"], row["Target"]
    if s in neuron_index and t in neuron_index:
        i, j = neuron_index[s], neuron_index[t]
        A[i, j] = A[j, i] = 1.0  # symmetric simplified graph

print(f"✅ Adjacency built: {A.shape}")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3️⃣  Prepare tensors (fresh rebuild)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
X = StandardScaler().fit_transform(expr_df.values)
y = motif_df.values.astype(np.float32)

X = torch.tensor(X, dtype=torch.float32)
y = torch.tensor(y, dtype=torch.float32)

device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
X, y, A = X.to(device), y.to(device), A.to(device)
print(f"✅ Rebuilt tensors: X={X.shape}, y={y.shape}, A={A.shape}, device={device}")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4️⃣  Define GNCA model
# ------------------------------------------------------------
class GNCA(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim, steps=3, dropout=0.3):
        super().__init__()
        self.fc_in = nn.Linear(in_dim, hidden_dim)
        self.fc_msg = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim)
        self.fc_out = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, out_dim)
        self.steps = steps
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)

    def forward(self, X, A):
        # X: [N, in_dim], A: [N, N]
        h = F.relu(self.fc_in(X))
        for _ in range(self.steps):
            msg = self.fc_msg(self.dropout(h))
            h = h + torch.matmul(A, msg) / (A.sum(1, keepdim=True) + 1)
            h = F.relu(h)
        return torch.sigmoid(self.fc_out(h))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5️⃣  Train GNCA
# ------------------------------------------------------------
model = GNCA(in_dim=X.shape[1], hidden_dim=64, out_dim=y.shape[1], steps=3, dropout=0.3).to(device)
opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)
loss_fn = nn.BCELoss()

best_loss, best_r = np.inf, 0
for epoch in range(1, 201):
    model.train()
    pred = model(X, A)
    loss = loss_fn(pred, y)
    opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); opt.step()

    if epoch % 20 == 0 or epoch == 1:
        model.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            r = np.corrcoef(pred.flatten().cpu(), y.flatten().cpu())[0, 1]
        print(f"Epoch {epoch:03d} | loss={loss.item():.4f} | r={r:.3f}")
        if loss.item() < best_loss:
            best_loss, best_r = loss.item(), r
            torch.save(model.state_dict(), EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_motif_best.pt")

print(f"\n✅ Training complete | Best loss={best_loss:.6f} | Final Pearson r={best_r:.3f}")


# [CELL] 
# ============================================================
# 🧩 Cell F — What the GNCA Learned
# ============================================================

import torch
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE
from scipy.stats import pearsonr

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1️⃣  Load best model & run final predictions
# ------------------------------------------------------------
model.load_state_dict(torch.load(EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_motif_best.pt", map_location=device))
model.eval()

with torch.no_grad():
    y_pred = model(X, A).cpu().numpy()
    y_true = y.cpu().numpy()

motif_names = list(motif_df.columns)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2️⃣  Per-motif performance metrics
# ------------------------------------------------------------
perf = []
for i, motif in enumerate(motif_names):
    r, _ = pearsonr(y_true[:, i], y_pred[:, i])
    mae = np.mean(np.abs(y_true[:, i] - y_pred[:, i]))
    perf.append((motif, r, mae))

perf_df = pd.DataFrame(perf, columns=["motif", "pearson_r", "mae"])
perf_df.to_csv(EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_motif_performance.csv", index=False)
print("✅ Saved per-motif performance → gnca_motif_performance.csv")
display(perf_df)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3️⃣  Visualize predictions vs. truth
# ------------------------------------------------------------
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(motif_names), figsize=(16, 3))
for i, motif in enumerate(motif_names):
    axes[i].scatter(y_true[:, i], y_pred[:, i], s=15, alpha=0.7)
    axes[i].plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "r--")
    axes[i].set_title(f"{motif}\nr={perf_df.iloc[i,1]:.2f}")
    axes[i].set_xlabel("True"); axes[i].set_ylabel("Pred")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4️⃣  Latent representation analysis
# ------------------------------------------------------------
with torch.no_grad():
    hidden = model.fc_in(X).cpu().numpy()  # first-layer embeddings

pca = PCA(n_components=2).fit_transform(hidden)
tsne = TSNE(n_components=2, perplexity=15, random_state=42).fit_transform(hidden)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))
ax[0].scatter(pca[:, 0], pca[:, 1], c=y_true[:, 0], cmap="viridis", s=20)
ax[0].set_title("PCA of hidden states (colored by motif 1)")
ax[1].scatter(tsne[:, 0], tsne[:, 1], c=y_true[:, 1], cmap="plasma", s=20)
ax[1].set_title("t-SNE of hidden states (colored by motif 2)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5️⃣  Saliency (feature influence) analysis
# ------------------------------------------------------------
X.requires_grad = True
model.eval()
pred_mean = model(X, A).mean()  # mean prediction scalar
pred_mean.backward()

saliency = X.grad.abs().mean(0).cpu().numpy()
sal_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "gene": expr_df.columns,
    "saliency": saliency
}).sort_values("saliency", ascending=False)

sal_df.to_csv(EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_saliency_genes.csv", index=False)
print("✅ Saved GNCA saliency scores → gnca_saliency_genes.csv")
print("Top 10 influential genes:")
display(sal_df.head(10))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6️⃣  Fixed Integrated Gradients function (PyTorch-safe)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
def integrated_gradients(model, X, A, baseline=None, steps=50):
    if baseline is None:
        baseline = torch.zeros_like(X)
    scaled_inputs = [baseline + (float(i)/steps) * (X - baseline) for i in range(steps + 1)]
    grads = []
    for inp in scaled_inputs:
        inp = inp.detach().clone().requires_grad_()
        out = model(inp, A).mean()
        out.backward()
        grads.append(inp.grad.detach().clone())
    grads = torch.stack(grads).mean(0)
    IG = ((X - baseline) * grads).mean(0).detach().cpu().numpy()   # ✅ detach before numpy()
    return IG



IG = integrated_gradients(model, X, A)
ig_df = pd.DataFrame({"gene": expr_df.columns, "integrated_gradients": IG})
ig_df = ig_df.sort_values("integrated_gradients", ascending=False)
ig_df.to_csv(EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_integrated_gradients_genes.csv", index=False)
print("✅ Saved integrated gradients → gnca_integrated_gradients_genes.csv")

display(ig_df.head(10))


# [CELL] 
# ============================================================
# 🧬 Cell G — Biological Context Mapping & Neurotransmitter Enrichment
# ============================================================
# Goal: Compare GNCA's top-ranked genes (saliency + IG) with known
# CeNGEN neurotransmitter and neuropeptide marker sets.

import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

EXPERIMENTS_OUT = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data/processed/experiments")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1️⃣  Load GNCA feature importance tables
# ------------------------------------------------------------
sal_path = EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_saliency_genes.csv"
ig_path  = EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_integrated_gradients_genes.csv"

sal_df = pd.read_csv(sal_path)
ig_df  = pd.read_csv(ig_path)

# Top-N selection
TOP_N = 100
sal_top = set(sal_df.head(TOP_N)["gene"])
ig_top  = set(ig_df.head(TOP_N)["gene"])
combined_top = sal_top.union(ig_top)
print(f"Loaded {len(sal_df)} saliency and {len(ig_df)} IG genes | {len(combined_top)} unique top candidates")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2️⃣  Define canonical neurotransmitter / neuropeptide marker sets
# ------------------------------------------------------------
marker_sets = {
    "serotonin": {
        "tph-1", "cat-4", "bas-1", "mod-5", "ser-4", "ser-1", "ser-7"
    },
    "dopamine": {
        "cat-2", "cat-4", "bas-1", "dat-1", "dop-1", "dop-2", "dop-3", "dop-4"
    },
    "acetylcholine": {
        "cha-1", "unc-17", "ace-1", "ace-2", "cho-1", "gar-1", "gar-2"
    },
    "GABA": {
        "unc-25", "unc-47", "snf-11", "gab-1", "exp-1", "lgc-35"
    },
    "glutamate": {
        "eat-4", "glr-1", "glr-2", "nmr-1", "avr-14", "avr-15"
    },
    "neuropeptide": {
        "flp-1", "flp-3", "flp-10", "nlp-1", "nlp-3", "nlp-12", "nlp-21"
    },
    "mechanosensory/adhesion": {
        "mec-3", "mec-4", "mec-7", "mec-10", "mec-17", "unc-6", "unc-40"
    }
}

# optional: mapping from WormBase IDs to names if file exists
map_path = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data/genomes/gene_name_map.tsv")
if map_path.exists():
    try:
        map_df = pd.read_csv(map_path, sep="\t")
        map_df.columns = [c.lower() for c in map_df.columns]
        wb_col = [c for c in map_df.columns if "wb" in c or "wormbase" in c][0]
        name_col = [c for c in map_df.columns if "gene" in c][0]
        id2name = dict(zip(map_df[wb_col], map_df[name_col]))
        # Map to gene names if possible
        combined_top_named = {id2name.get(g, g) for g in combined_top}
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"⚠️ Mapping skipped ({e})")
        combined_top_named = combined_top
else:
    combined_top_named = combined_top

# ------------------------------------------------------------

# ------------------------------------------------------------
enrichment = []
for system, markers in marker_sets.items():
    hits = [g for g in combined_top_named if g.lower() in markers]
    enrichment.append({
        "system": system,
        "hits": len(hits),
        "hit_genes": ", ".join(hits)
    })

# [CELL] 
# ============================================================
# 📘 Cell G2 — Map WormBase IDs → gene names, then re-run enrichment
# ============================================================
import re, pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

DATA_ROOT = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data")
EXPERIMENTS_OUT = DATA_ROOT / "processed" / "experiments"

sal_path = EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_saliency_genes.csv"
ig_path  = EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_integrated_gradients_genes.csv"

sal_df = pd.read_csv(sal_path)
ig_df  = pd.read_csv(ig_path)

# ---------- 1) Build WBGene -> gene_name map from any GTF/GFF3 ----------
def parse_attrs(attr_str):
    # Works for both GTF (key "gene_name"/"gene_id") and GFF3 (key "Name"/"ID")
    d = {}
    # split on ; and strip
    for kv in re.split(r';\s*', str(attr_str).strip('; ')):
        if not kv: continue
        if '=' in kv: k,v = kv.split('=',1)
        elif ' ' in kv: k,v = kv.split(' ',1)
        else: continue
        d[k.strip().strip('"')] = v.strip().strip('"')
    return d

def collect_maps():
    wb2name = {}
    # look in common places
    candidates = []
    for sub in ["processed/gtf", "processed/gff3", "genomes", "processed/genomes", "raw/gtf", "raw/gff3"]:
        p = DATA_ROOT / sub
        if p.exists():
            candidates += list(p.rglob("*.gtf")) + list(p.rglob("*.gff3"))
    # deduplicate while keeping order
    seen = set(); gt_files = []
    for f in candidates:
        if f not in seen:
            gt_files.append(f); seen.add(f)
    if not gt_files:
        print("⚠️ No GTF/GFF3 found under data/, mapping will be empty.")
        return {}
    # parse a few files until we get a good number of mappings
    for gt in gt_files:
        try:
            print(f"Parsing: {gt.name}")
            with gt.open() as fh:
                for line in fh:
                    if line.startswith("#"): continue
                    parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
                    if len(parts) < 9: continue
                    attrs = parse_attrs(parts[8])
                    # Try both formats
                    wb = attrs.get("gene_id") or attrs.get("gene") or attrs.get("ID")
                    name = attrs.get("gene_name") or attrs.get("Name") or attrs.get("gene")
                    # Keep only proper WBGene IDs and decent names
                    if wb and re.match(r"^WBGene\d{8}$", wb):
                        if name and not re.match(r"^WBGene\d{8}$", name):
                            wb2name.setdefault(wb, name)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  (skip {gt.name}: {e})")
        if len(wb2name) > 5000:  # enough
            break
    print(f"✅ built map size: {len(wb2name)}")
    return wb2name

wb2name = collect_maps()

# Fallback: if still empty, try a simple heuristic (strip version suffixes etc.)
if not wb2name:
    print("⚠️ Map empty; will pass through WBGene IDs unchanged.")
    def to_name(x): return x
else:
    def to_name(x): return wb2name.get(x, x)

# ---------- 2) Map top-N features to gene names ----------
TOP_N = 100
sal_top = sal_df.head(TOP_N)["gene"].map(to_name)
ig_top  = ig_df.head(TOP_N)["gene"].map(to_name)
combined_top = set(sal_top).union(set(ig_top))
print(f"Top set size after mapping: {len(combined_top)}")

# ---------- 3) Define marker sets (gene names, lowercase) ----------
marker_sets = {
    "serotonin": {"tph-1","cat-4","bas-1","mod-5","ser-4","ser-1","ser-7"},
    "dopamine": {"cat-2","cat-4","bas-1","dat-1","dop-1","dop-2","dop-3","dop-4"},
    "acetylcholine": {"cha-1","unc-17","ace-1","ace-2","cho-1","gar-1","gar-2"},
    "GABA": {"unc-25","unc-47","snf-11","gab-1","exp-1","lgc-35"},
    "glutamate": {"eat-4","glr-1","glr-2","nmr-1","avr-14","avr-15"},
    "neuropeptide": {"flp-1","flp-3","flp-10","nlp-1","nlp-3","nlp-12","nlp-21"},
    "mechanosensory/adhesion": {"mec-3","mec-4","mec-7","mec-10","mec-17","unc-6","unc-40"},
}
combined_lc = {str(g).lower() for g in combined_top}

# ---------- 4) Enrichment counts ----------
rows = []
for system, markers in marker_sets.items():
    hits = sorted(combined_lc & {m.lower() for m in markers})
    rows.append({"system": system, "hits": len(hits), "hit_genes": ", ".join(hits)})
enrich_df = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("hits", ascending=False)
display(enrich_df)

# ---------- 5) Plot ----------
plt.figure(figsize=(8,4))
plt.barh(enrich_df["system"], enrich_df["hits"])
plt.gca().invert_yaxis()
plt.xlabel(f"Hits in top-{TOP_N} GNCA genes (mapped to names)")
plt.title("Neurotransmitter/Peptide Enrichment")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Save
out_path = EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_neurotransmitter_enrichment_mapped.csv"
enrich_df.to_csv(out_path, index=False)
print(f"✅ Saved → {out_path}")


# [CELL] 
# ============================================================
# 📘 Cell H (final robust) — GO / KEGG Biological Enrichment
# ============================================================

import pandas as pd
import mygene
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from collections import Counter
from pathlib import Path
import time

EXPERIMENTS_OUT = Path("/mnt/ssd4tb/Desktop/C-Elegans/data/processed/experiments")

# 1️⃣ Load top genes
sal_df = pd.read_csv(EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_saliency_genes.csv")
ig_df  = pd.read_csv(EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_integrated_gradients_genes.csv")

TOP_N = 200
top_genes = pd.concat([
    sal_df.head(TOP_N)["gene"],
    ig_df.head(TOP_N)["gene"]
]).drop_duplicates().tolist()
print(f"Analyzing {len(top_genes)} top-ranked GNCA genes")

# 2️⃣ Query MyGene.info
mg = mygene.MyGeneInfo()
fields = "go.BP,go.MF,go.CC,pathway.kegg"
species_id = 6239  # C. elegans

try:
    results = mg.querymany(top_genes, scopes="wormbase", species=species_id,
                           fields=fields, as_dataframe=True, verbose=False)
except Exception as e:
    print(f"⚠️ Query failed ({e}); retrying with broader scopes ...")
    time.sleep(2)
    results = mg.querymany(top_genes, scopes="symbol,entrezgene,ensemblgene",
                           species=species_id, fields=fields, as_dataframe=True, verbose=False)

print("✅ Retrieved annotations with columns:", list(results.columns))

# 3️⃣  Helper to flatten any format
def flatten_terms(col_candidates):
    """Extract all term names from whichever column exists."""
    for c in col_candidates:
        if c in results.columns:
            vals = results[c].dropna().values
            out = []
            for v in vals:
                if isinstance(v, list):
                    for item in v:
                        if isinstance(item, dict):
                            out.append(item.get("term") or item.get("name") or str(item))
                        else:
                            out.append(str(item))
                elif isinstance(v, dict):
                    out.append(v.get("term") or v.get("name") or str(v))
                else:
                    out.append(str(v))
            return out
    return []

# 4️⃣  Extract and count
go_bp = flatten_terms(["go.BP.name", "go.BP"])
go_mf = flatten_terms(["go.MF.name", "go.MF"])
go_cc = flatten_terms(["go.CC.name", "go.CC"])
kegg  = flatten_terms(["pathway.kegg.name", "pathway.kegg"])

def top_terms(term_list, label, n=15):
    if not term_list:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=[label, "count"])
    counts = Counter(term_list)
    df = pd.DataFrame(counts.most_common(n), columns=[label, "count"])
    return df

bp_df = top_terms(go_bp, "GO Biological Process")
mf_df = top_terms(go_mf, "GO Molecular Function")
cc_df = top_terms(go_cc, "GO Cellular Component")
kg_df = top_terms(kegg,  "KEGG Pathway")

# 5️⃣ Plot
def plot_enrichment(df, title, color):
    if df.empty:
        print(f"(no {title} terms found)")
        return
    plt.figure(figsize=(8,4))
    plt.barh(df.iloc[::-1,0], df.iloc[::-1,1], color=color)
    plt.xlabel("Frequency among top genes")
    plt.title(title)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

plot_enrichment(bp_df, "GO: Biological Process", "seagreen")
plot_enrichment(mf_df, "GO: Molecular Function", "mediumblue")
plot_enrichment(cc_df, "GO: Cellular Component", "purple")
plot_enrichment(kg_df, "KEGG Pathway", "orange")

# 6️⃣ Save
out_path = EXPERIMENTS_OUT / "gnca_go_kegg_enrichment_final.xlsx"
with pd.ExcelWriter(out_path) as writer:
    bp_df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name="GO_BP", index=False)
    mf_df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name="GO_MF", index=False)
    cc_df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name="GO_CC", index=False)
    kg_df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name="KEGG", index=False)
print(f"✅ Saved enrichment tables → {out_path}")


# [CELL] 
# CELL 0: Config & utils
import os, json, math, random, gc, time
from dataclasses import dataclass
import numpy as np, pandas as pd, networkx as nx

import torch
from torch import nn
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader

RNG_SEED = 1337
random.seed(RNG_SEED); np.random.seed(RNG_SEED); torch.manual_seed(RNG_SEED)

@dataclass
class CFG:
    ROOT: str = "./"
    DATA: str = "./DATA_PROCESSED"
    OUT: str = "./ARTIFACTS"
    BATCH: int = 64
    EPOCHS: int = 150
    LR: float = 2e-3
    WD: float = 1e-3
    STEPS: int = 8           # NCA unroll steps
    HIDDEN: int = 64
    DROPOUT: float = 0.10
    KFOLDS: int = 5
    DEVICE: str = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"

os.makedirs(CFG.OUT, exist_ok=True)

def save_json(obj, path):
    with open(path, "w") as f: json.dump(obj, f, indent=2)

def bootstrap_ci(arr, iters=2000, alpha=0.05):
    arr = np.asarray(arr)
    n = len(arr)
    boots = []
    for _ in range(iters):
        s = np.random.choice(arr, size=n, replace=True)
        boots.append(np.mean(s))
    lo = np.quantile(boots, alpha/2); hi = np.quantile(boots, 1-alpha/2)
    return float(np.mean(arr)), float(lo), float(hi)

def spearmanr(x, y):
    rx = pd.Series(x).rank().values
    ry = pd.Series(y).rank().values
    return np.corrcoef(rx, ry)[0,1]


# [CELL] 
# --- PATCHED MOTIF COMPUTATION (robust to missing nodes) ---

# Remove neuron names not in the actual graph
neurons_in_graph = [n for n in neurons if n in G.nodes()]
missing = set(neurons) - set(neurons_in_graph)
if missing:
    print(f"⚠️ {len(missing)} neuron IDs not in graph — dropping: {sorted(list(missing))[:10]}{'...' if len(missing)>10 else ''}")
neurons = neurons_in_graph  # update canonical neuron list

def motif_counts_safe(G, neurons):
    idx = {n:i for i,n in enumerate(neurons)}
    M = np.zeros((len(neurons),5),dtype=np.float32)
    for u,v in G.edges():
        if u not in idx or v not in idx: 
            continue
        if G.has_edge(v,u):
            M[idx[u],0]+=1; M[idx[v],0]+=1
    for a in neurons:
        outs = set(G.successors(a)) if a in G else set()
        ins  = set(G.predecessors(a)) if a in G else set()
        if len(outs)>1: M[idx[a],3]+=len(outs)
        if len(ins)>1:  M[idx[a],4]+=len(ins)
        for b in outs:
            if b not in G: continue
            outs_b = set(G.successors(b))
            M[idx[a],1]+=len(outs_b)
            M[idx[a],2]+=len(outs.intersection(outs_b))
    return (M - M.mean(0)) / (M.std(0)+1e-6)

motifs = motif_counts_safe(G, neurons)
print(f"✅ Motif matrix built successfully: {motifs.shape}")


# [CELL] 
# === FULL SYNC + SAFE MOTIF REBUILD ===

print(f"Before sync: {len(neurons)} neurons listed, {G.number_of_nodes()} nodes in graph.")

# keep only neurons actually present in the graph
neurons = [n for n in neurons if n in G.nodes()]
print(f"✅ After sync: {len(neurons)} neurons retained inside graph.")

# restrict degree table and expression to same index
deg_df = deg_df.loc[deg_df.index.intersection(neurons)]
expr   = expr.loc[expr.index.intersection(neurons)]

def motif_counts_synced(G, neurons):
    """Compute basic motif participation counts safely."""
    idx = {n:i for i,n in enumerate(neurons)}
    M = np.zeros((len(neurons),5),dtype=np.float32)
    # 0 reciprocal, 1 chain, 2 feedforward, 3 divergent, 4 convergent
    for u,v in G.edges():
        if u not in idx or v not in idx: continue
        if G.has_edge(v,u):
            M[idx[u],0]+=1; M[idx[v],0]+=1
    for a in neurons:
        outs = set(G.successors(a)) if a in G else set()
        ins  = set(G.predecessors(a)) if a in G else set()
        if len(outs)>1: M[idx[a],3]+=len(outs)
        if len(ins)>1:  M[idx[a],4]+=len(ins)
        for b in outs:
            if b not in G: continue
            outs_b = set(G.successors(b))
            M[idx[a],1]+=len(outs_b)
            M[idx[a],2]+=len(outs.intersection(outs_b))
    return (M - M.mean(0)) / (M.std(0)+1e-6)

motifs = motif_counts_synced(G, neurons)
print(f"✅ Motif matrix rebuilt: {motifs.shape}")


# [CELL] 
# === UNIVERSAL CONTINUATION BLOCK ===
# Integrates with previous NCA notebook variables
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, networkx as nx, torch, torch.nn as nn
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, roc_auc_score, average_precision_score, f1_score

# --- 1. Auto-detect existing variables ---
def detect_existing_data():
    g, e, x = None, None, None
    globals_ = globals()
    for cand in ['edges','edge_df','E','connectome_edges','edges_df']:
        if cand in globals_:
            e = globals_[cand]
            break
    for cand in ['expr','expression','expr_df','X','cengen_expr']:
        if cand in globals_:
            x = globals_[cand]
            break
    for cand in ['G','graph','connectome_graph','nxG']:
        if cand in globals_:
            g = globals_[cand]
            break
    return x,e,g

expr, edges, G_existing = detect_existing_data()
assert expr is not None, "Expression dataframe (expr) not found—please ensure your existing variable is named expr."
assert edges is not None, "Edges dataframe (edges) not found—please ensure your existing variable is named edges."

# --- 2. Canonicalize neuron IDs ---
def canon(s): return str(s).strip().upper().replace("  "," ")
edges = edges.copy()
edges.columns = [c.lower().strip() for c in edges.columns]
if 'source' not in edges.columns or 'target' not in edges.columns:
    for c in edges.columns:
        if 'pre' in c: edges = edges.rename(columns={c:'source'})
        if 'post' in c: edges = edges.rename(columns={c:'target'})
expr = expr.copy()
edges['source'] = edges['source'].map(canon)
edges['target'] = edges['target'].map(canon)
expr.index = expr.index.map(canon)
neurons = sorted(set(edges['source']).union(edges['target']))
expr = expr.loc[expr.index.intersection(neurons)]
print(f"✅ Using {expr.shape[0]} neurons × {expr.shape[1]} genes; {len(edges)} edges")

# --- 3. Build base graph if not present ---
if G_existing is None:
    G = nx.DiGraph()
    for _,r in edges.iterrows():
        G.add_edge(r['source'], r['target'], w=float(r.get('synapsecount',1)))
else:
    G = G_existing

# --- PATCHED DEGREE FEATURE BUILDER ---

# --- FINAL PATCHED DEGREE FEATURE BUILDER (robust for any graph type) ---

in_deg = []
out_deg = []

for n in neurons:
    # compute weighted degrees if weights exist
    indeg_val = 0.0
    outdeg_val = 0.0

    # Defensive: iterate edges manually to avoid view objects
    if G.is_directed():
        # incoming edges
        for _, src, data in G.in_edges(n, data=True):
            indeg_val += float(data.get("w", 1.0))
        # outgoing edges
        for _, tgt, data in G.out_edges(n, data=True):
            outdeg_val += float(data.get("w", 1.0))
    else:
        # undirected fallback
        for _, _, data in G.edges(n, data=True):
            indeg_val += float(data.get("w", 1.0))
            outdeg_val = indeg_val

    in_deg.append(indeg_val)
    out_deg.append(outdeg_val)

deg_df = pd.DataFrame({"in_deg": in_deg, "out_deg": out_deg}, index=neurons)
deg_df = (deg_df - deg_df.mean(numeric_only=True)) / (deg_df.std(numeric_only=True) + 1e-6)

print(f"✅ Degree features built: {deg_df.shape}")
display(deg_df.head())


# --- FINAL MOTIF CLEANUP AND SAFEGUARD ---
# Remove old motif_counts from memory so it can’t be accidentally called
if 'motif_counts' in globals():
    del motif_counts

# Ensure neurons, expr, deg_df all aligned to graph
neurons = [n for n in expr.index if n in G.nodes()]
expr   = expr.loc[neurons]
deg_df = deg_df.loc[deg_df.index.intersection(neurons)]

# Define the single correct motif function
def motif_counts_safe(G, neurons):
    idx = {n:i for i,n in enumerate(neurons)}
    M = np.zeros((len(neurons),5),dtype=np.float32)
    for u,v in G.edges():
        if u not in idx or v not in idx: continue
        if G.has_edge(v,u):
            M[idx[u],0]+=1; M[idx[v],0]+=1
    for a in neurons:
        if a not in G: continue
        outs = set(G.successors(a))
        ins  = set(G.predecessors(a))
        if len(outs)>1: M[idx[a],3]+=len(outs)
        if len(ins)>1:  M[idx[a],4]+=len(ins)
        for b in outs:
            if b not in G: continue
            outs_b = set(G.successors(b))
            M[idx[a],1]+=len(outs_b)
            M[idx[a],2]+=len(outs.intersection(outs_b))
    return (M - M.mean(0)) / (M.std(0)+1e-6)

motifs = motif_counts_safe(G, neurons)
print(f"✅ Final motif matrix ready: {motifs.shape}")

# Confirm integrity
print(f"All matrices aligned — expr: {expr.shape}, deg_df: {deg_df.shape}, motifs: {motifs.shape}")

# --- 6. Merge all features ---
Xg = (expr - expr.mean())/(expr.std()+1e-6)
X = np.concatenate([Xg.values, deg_df.values, motifs], axis=1).astype(np.float32)
print(f"✅ Feature tensor: {X.shape}")

# --- 7. Quick sanity test: predict motif participation using simple MLP ---
class TinyNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, indim, outdim=5):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(indim,128), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(128,64), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(64,outdim)
        )
    def forward(self,x): return self.net(x)

model = TinyNN(X.shape[1], motifs.shape[1])
opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=2e-3)
crit = nn.MSELoss()

Xt = torch.tensor(X, dtype=torch.float32)
yt = torch.tensor(motifs, dtype=torch.float32)

for epoch in range(80):
    model.train(); opt.zero_grad()
    pred = model(Xt)
    loss = crit(pred, yt)
    loss.backward(); opt.step()
print(f"✅ Quick test loss: {loss.item():.4f}")

# --- 8. Ready state for Tasks A–D ---
globals().update({"expr":expr,"edges":edges,"neurons":neurons,"G":G,"deg_df":deg_df,"motifs":motifs,"X":X})
print("Environment primed for Tasks A–D.")


# [CELL] 
# === TASK A: Predict Motif Participation (per-neuron regression) ===
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error
import numpy as np, torch, torch.nn as nn

kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=1337)
X_np = X
y_np = motifs

def run_fold(train_idx, val_idx, tag):
    model = nn.Sequential(
        nn.Linear(X_np.shape[1], 128), nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Linear(128, 64), nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Linear(64, y_np.shape[1])
    )
    opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=2e-3)
    crit = nn.MSELoss()
    Xt = torch.tensor(X_np[train_idx], dtype=torch.float32)
    yt = torch.tensor(y_np[train_idx], dtype=torch.float32)
    Xv = torch.tensor(X_np[val_idx], dtype=torch.float32)
    yv = torch.tensor(y_np[val_idx], dtype=torch.float32)

    for ep in range(100):
        model.train(); opt.zero_grad()
        loss = crit(model(Xt), yt)
        loss.backward(); opt.step()
    model.eval()
    with torch.no_grad():
        pv = model(Xv).numpy()
    spearman = np.corrcoef(yv.flatten(), pv.flatten())[0,1]
    mae = mean_absolute_error(yv.flatten(), pv.flatten())
    return {"tag":tag, "r":spearman, "mae":mae}

folds = []
for i,(tr,va) in enumerate(kf.split(X_np),1):
    res = run_fold(tr,va,f"fold{i}")
    print(res); folds.append(res)
print("Average r:", np.mean([f["r"] for f in folds]))


# [CELL] 
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold
kf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=1337)

# relaxed peptide threshold
y_pep = (zexpr[peptide_genes].max(1) > 0.5).astype(int).values
y_sign = (score >= -0.2).astype(int).values  # widen the margin

def safe_run_foldB(tr, va, X, y):
    if len(np.unique(y[va])) < 2:
        return {"auc":np.nan,"ap":np.nan,"f1":np.nan}
    # same as before …


# [CELL] 
# === TASK B: Predict Neuropeptide Expression (binary) ===
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, average_precision_score, f1_score

peptide_genes = [c for c in expr.columns if c.startswith(("NLP-","FLP-","INS-"))]
zexpr = (expr - expr.mean()) / (expr.std()+1e-6)
y_pep = (zexpr[peptide_genes].max(1) > 1.0).astype(int).values

kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=1337)
def run_foldB(tr,va):
    model = nn.Sequential(
        nn.Linear(X.shape[1],128), nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Linear(128,64), nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Linear(64,1)
    )
    opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=2e-3)
    crit = nn.BCEWithLogitsLoss()
    Xt = torch.tensor(X[tr],dtype=torch.float32)
    yt = torch.tensor(y_pep[tr,None],dtype=torch.float32)
    Xv = torch.tensor(X[va],dtype=torch.float32)
    yv = y_pep[va]
    for ep in range(80):
        model.train(); opt.zero_grad()
        loss = crit(model(Xt),yt); loss.backward(); opt.step()
    model.eval(); p = torch.sigmoid(model(Xv)).detach().numpy().ravel()
    return {
        "auc":roc_auc_score(yv,p),
        "ap":average_precision_score(yv,p),
        "f1":f1_score(yv,(p>0.5).astype(int))
    }

scores=[run_foldB(*split) for split in kf.split(X)]
print("Mean AUC:",np.mean([s["auc"] for s in scores]))


# [CELL] 
# === TASK C: Predict Synapse Sign (proxy) ===
inh = [c for c in expr.columns if c.startswith(("UNC-25","GAD","VGAT","SNF-11"))]
exc = [c for c in expr.columns if c.startswith(("CHA-1","UNC-17","EAT-4","GLT"))]
score = zexpr[exc].mean(1).fillna(0) - zexpr[inh].mean(1).fillna(0)
y_sign = (score >= 0).astype(int).values

scoresC=[run_foldB(*split) for split in kf.split(X)]
print("Mean AUC (sign):",np.mean([s["auc"] for s in scoresC]))


# [CELL] 
# === TASK D: Predict Behavioral Role (3-class) ===
sensory = [n for n in neurons if n.startswith(("AF","AL","AS","AVM","AWA","AWC","ASE","ASH","URX","OL"))]
motor   = [n for n in neurons if n.startswith(("DA","DB","DD","VA","VB","VD","AS"))]
role = []
for n in neurons:
    if n in sensory: role.append(0)         # sensory
    elif n in motor: role.append(2)         # motor
    else: role.append(1)                    # interneuron
y_role = np.array(role)

def run_foldD(tr,va):
    model = nn.Sequential(
        nn.Linear(X.shape[1],128), nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Linear(128,64), nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Linear(64,3)
    )
    opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=2e-3)
    crit = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    Xt = torch.tensor(X[tr],dtype=torch.float32)
    yt = torch.tensor(y_role[tr],dtype=torch.long)
    Xv = torch.tensor(X[va],dtype=torch.float32)
    yv = y_role[va]
    for ep in range(100):
        model.train(); opt.zero_grad()
        loss = crit(model(Xt),yt); loss.backward(); opt.step()
    model.eval()
    with torch.no_grad():
        pv = model(Xv).numpy()
    acc = (pv.argmax(1)==yv).mean()
    return {"acc":acc}

scoresD=[run_foldD(*split) for split in kf.split(X)]
print("Mean Accuracy (role):",np.mean([s["acc"] for s in scoresD]))

plt.bar(range(len(metrics)), metrics)

plt.xlabel("Fold")
plt.title("Task A – Motif participation correlation per fold")
plt.show()

# [CELL] 
# ==== CELL: Variance partitioning for Motifs (regression) & Role (classification) ====
import numpy as np, pandas as pd
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge, LogisticRegression
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score, accuracy_score

# helpers
def zscore(A): return (A - A.mean(0, keepdims=True)) / (A.std(0, keepdims=True) + 1e-6)

# Features already available:
X_expr = zscore(expr.values.astype(np.float32))                  # (N, G)
X_deg  = zscore(deg_df.reindex(expr.index).values.astype(float)) # (N, 2)
Y_motif = motifs.astype(np.float32)                               # (N, 5)

# Behavior labels (rebuild if missing)
try:
    y_role  # exists
except NameError:
    sensory = [n for n in neurons if n.startswith(("AF","AL","AS","AVM","AWA","AWC","ASE","ASH","URX","OL"))]
    motor   = [n for n in neurons if n.startswith(("DA","DB","DD","VA","VB","VD","AS"))]
    role = []
    for n in neurons:
        if n in sensory: role.append(0)
        elif n in motor: role.append(2)
        else: role.append(1)
    y_role = np.array(role)

kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=1337)

def motif_partition():
    res = {"expr":[], "deg":[], "both":[], "expr_partial":[], "deg_partial":[]}
    for tr, va in kf.split(X_expr):
        # train
        m_expr = Ridge(alpha=1.0).fit(X_expr[tr], Y_motif[tr])
        m_deg  = Ridge(alpha=1.0).fit(X_deg[tr],  Y_motif[tr])
        m_both = Ridge(alpha=1.0).fit(np.hstack([X_expr[tr], X_deg[tr]]), Y_motif[tr])
        # predict
        yv = Y_motif[va]
        r2_e = r2_score(yv, m_expr.predict(X_expr[va]))
        r2_d = r2_score(yv, m_deg.predict(X_deg[va]))
        r2_b = r2_score(yv, m_both.predict(np.hstack([X_expr[va], X_deg[va]])))
        res["expr"].append(r2_e); res["deg"].append(r2_d); res["both"].append(r2_b)
        # partial contributions
        res["expr_partial"].append(max(0.0, r2_b - r2_d))
        res["deg_partial"].append(max(0.0, r2_b - r2_e))
    return {k:(float(np.mean(v)), float(np.std(v))) for k,v in res.items()}

def role_partition():
    res = {"expr":[], "deg":[], "both":[], "expr_partial":[], "deg_partial":[]}
    for tr, va in kf.split(X_expr):
        # classifiers
        c_expr = LogisticRegression(max_iter=200, multi_class="multinomial").fit(X_expr[tr], y_role[tr])
        c_deg  = LogisticRegression(max_iter=200, multi_class="multinomial").fit(X_deg[tr],  y_role[tr])
        c_both = LogisticRegression(max_iter=200, multi_class="multinomial").fit(np.hstack([X_expr[tr], X_deg[tr]]), y_role[tr])
        yv = y_role[va]
        a_e = accuracy_score(yv, c_expr.predict(X_expr[va]))
        a_d = accuracy_score(yv, c_deg.predict(X_deg[va]))
        a_b = accuracy_score(yv, c_both.predict(np.hstack([X_expr[va], X_deg[va]])))
        res["expr"].append(a_e); res["deg"].append(a_d); res["both"].append(a_b)
        # partial (Δaccuracy)
        res["expr_partial"].append(max(0.0, a_b - a_d))
        res["deg_partial"].append(max(0.0, a_b - a_e))
    return {k:(float(np.mean(v)), float(np.std(v))) for k,v in res.items()}

motif_var = motif_partition()
role_var  = role_partition()
print("== Motif variance partition (R² mean±sd) =="); print(motif_var)
print("\n== Role variance partition (Acc mean±sd) =="); print(role_var)


# [CELL] 
# ==== CELL: Mediation test (Expression -> Motif -> Role) ====
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from scipy.stats import norm

# Reduce dimensionality for a stable test
p_expr = PCA(n_components=16, random_state=0).fit_transform(X_expr)
p_motif= PCA(n_components=3,  random_state=0).fit_transform(Y_motif)

# Encode role as 1-vs-rest for a smooth mediation proxy (choose "motor" as 1)
y_motor = (y_role==2).astype(float)

# Paths:
# a: expr -> motif (regress each motif PC on expr PCs, take mean beta magnitude)
a_model = LinearRegression().fit(p_expr, p_motif)
a_hat   = a_model.coef_.mean()  # coarse scalar proxy of a-path strength

# b & c': role ~ expr + motif (linear prob. proxy)
XZ = np.hstack([p_expr, p_motif])
bc_model = LinearRegression().fit(XZ, y_motor)
# Split coefficients
b_hat = np.mean(bc_model.coef_[-p_motif.shape[1]:])   # motif part
c_prime_hat = np.mean(bc_model.coef_[:p_expr.shape[1]])  # direct expr part

# Sobel test for indirect effect (a*b)
# (Use rough var estimates from residuals; fine for signal check.)
res_a = p_motif - a_model.predict(p_expr)
res_b = y_motor - bc_model.predict(XZ)
sa = res_a.std(); sb = res_b.std()
sobel_se = np.sqrt((b_hat**2)*(sa.mean()**2) + (a_hat**2)*(sb**2))
z = (a_hat*b_hat) / (sobel_se + 1e-8)
p = 2*(1 - norm.cdf(abs(z)))

print(f"Indirect effect a*b ≈ {a_hat*b_hat:.4f}, Sobel z≈{z:.2f}, p≈{p:.3g}")
print(f"Direct expr effect c' ≈ {c_prime_hat:.4f}  (should be smaller than total expr-only model)")


# [CELL] 
# ==== CELL: Degree-binned permutation for Task B/C or Role ====
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
import numpy as np, pandas as pd

deg_cont = deg_df["out_deg"].reindex(expr.index).values  # one structural scalar
def degree_bins(v, nbins=5):
    return pd.qcut(v, q=min(nbins, len(np.unique(v))), duplicates="drop").astype(str).values

def perm_pvalue_auc(y_true, y_score, deg, iters=2000, seed=1337):
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    bins = degree_bins(deg)
    auc_obs = roc_auc_score(y_true, y_score)
    ge = 0
    for _ in range(iters):
        y_perm = y_true.copy()
        for b in np.unique(bins):
            idx = np.where(bins==b)[0]
            rng.shuffle(y_perm[idx])
        auc_p = roc_auc_score(y_perm, y_score)
        ge += (auc_p >= auc_obs)
    return auc_obs, (ge+1)/(iters+1)

print("Example usage: pass your y_true and model scores from Task B/C here to get degree-aware p-values.")


# [CELL] 
# ==== CELL: Residual pathway test (behavior) ====
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score

# Reduce dims for stability
p_expr = PCA(n_components=16, random_state=0).fit_transform(X_expr)
p_motif= PCA(n_components=3,  random_state=0).fit_transform(Y_motif)

# Step 1: predict behavior from expression alone
lr1 = LinearRegression().fit(p_expr, y_role)  # treating labels as ordinal proxy
r2_expr_only = r2_score(y_role, lr1.predict(p_expr))

# Step 2: regress expression effect out of behavior, then try to predict residual from expression again
beh_resid = y_role - lr1.predict(p_expr)
lr2 = LinearRegression().fit(p_expr, beh_resid)
r2_expr_on_resid = r2_score(beh_resid, lr2.predict(p_expr))

# Step 3: predict behavior from motifs alone
lr3 = LinearRegression().fit(p_motif, y_role)
r2_motif = r2_score(y_role, lr3.predict(p_motif))

print(f"R²(behavior ~ expr) = {r2_expr_only:.3f}")
print(f"R²(residual after expr ~ expr) = {r2_expr_on_resid:.3f}  (should drop to ~0)")
print(f"R²(behavior ~ motif) = {r2_motif:.3f}  (should be notably higher)")


# [CELL] 
# ==== CELL: CCA alignment between expression and motif spaces ====
from sklearn.cross_decomposition import CCA

p_expr = PCA(n_components=50, random_state=0).fit_transform(X_expr)
p_mtf  = PCA(n_components=5,  random_state=0).fit_transform(Y_motif)

cca = CCA(n_components=3, max_iter=1000)
U, V = cca.fit_transform(p_expr, p_mtf)
cc = [np.corrcoef(U[:,i], V[:,i])[0,1] for i in range(U.shape[1])]
print("Canonical correlations (expr <-> motif):", [round(float(c),3) for c in cc])


# [CELL] 
# ==== CELL: In-silico gene KO experiment (ΔMotif vs ΔRole) ====
import numpy as np
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

# pick top-variance genes as candidates (or your prior list)
gene_var = expr.var(0).sort_values(ascending=False)
ko_genes = list(gene_var.head(20).index)[:10]  # 10 genes

# small train/val split
tr_idx, va_idx = train_test_split(np.arange(len(neurons)), test_size=0.3, random_state=42, stratify=y_role)

def fit_motif_regressor(Xe):
    model = Ridge(alpha=1.0).fit(np.hstack([Xe[tr_idx], X_deg[tr_idx]]), Y_motif[tr_idx])
    pv = model.predict(np.hstack([Xe[va_idx], X_deg[va_idx]]))
    r2 = r2_score(Y_motif[va_idx], pv)
    return r2

def fit_role_classifier(Xe):
    clf = LogisticRegression(max_iter=300, multi_class="multinomial").fit(np.hstack([Xe[tr_idx], X_deg[tr_idx]]), y_role[tr_idx])
    acc = accuracy_score(y_role[va_idx], clf.predict(np.hstack([Xe[va_idx], X_deg[va_idx]])))
    return acc

# baseline
Xe_base = X_expr.copy()
base_motif = fit_motif_regressor(Xe_base)
base_role  = fit_role_classifier(Xe_base)

effects = []
for g in ko_genes:
    Xe = Xe_base.copy()
    j = expr.columns.get_loc(g)
    Xe[:, j] = 0.0  # gene "KO"
    effects.append({
        "gene": g,
        "dR2_motif": fit_motif_regressor(Xe) - base_motif,
        "dACC_role":  fit_role_classifier(Xe) - base_role
    })

effects_df = pd.DataFrame(effects).sort_values("dR2_motif")
print("KO effects (negative means performance drop):")
display(effects_df)


# [CELL] 
# ==== NON-LINEAR MEDIATION: Expr -> Motif -> Role ====
import numpy as np, torch, torch.nn as nn
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score

torch.manual_seed(1337); np.random.seed(1337)

# Dimensionality reduction for stability
pca_expr = PCA(n_components=64, random_state=0)
Zx = pca_expr.fit_transform((expr - expr.mean())/(expr.std()+1e-6)).astype(np.float32)   # (N, 64)
Zm = PCA(n_components=5, random_state=0).fit_transform(motifs).astype(np.float32)       # (N, 5)
y = y_role.astype(int)  # 0/1/2

class Enc(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_in=64, d_out=5):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(d_in,128), nn.ReLU(),
                                 nn.Linear(128,64), nn.ReLU(),
                                 nn.Linear(64,d_out))
    def forward(self,x): return self.net(x)

class Clf(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_in=5):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(d_in,64), nn.ReLU(),
                                 nn.Linear(64,3))
    def forward(self,m): return self.net(m)

def softmax_acc(logits, y_true):
    """
    Computes simple classification accuracy.
    logits: (N, num_classes)
    y_true: (N,)
    """
    yhat = logits.argmax(1)
    return (yhat == y_true).float().mean().item()


kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=1337)
indirect_effects = []; direct_effects = []; accs = []

for tr, va in kf.split(Zx):
    # Train encoder: Expr -> Motif (nonlinear)
    enc = Enc().float()
    opt = torch.optim.Adam(enc.parameters(), lr=2e-3)
    crit = nn.MSELoss()
    Xt = torch.tensor(Zx[tr]); Mt = torch.tensor(Zm[tr])
    Xv = torch.tensor(Zx[va]); Mv = torch.tensor(Zm[va])
    for ep in range(120):
        opt.zero_grad(); loss = crit(enc(Xt), Mt); loss.backward(); opt.step()

    # Train classifier: Motif -> Role (nonlinear)
    clf = Clf().float()
    opt2 = torch.optim.Adam(clf.parameters(), lr=2e-3)
    crit2 = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    Yt = torch.tensor(y[tr]); Yv = torch.tensor(y[va])
    for ep in range(120):
        opt2.zero_grad(); loss2 = crit2(clf(Mt), Yt); loss2.backward(); opt2.step()

    # ACC with true motifs
    acc_true = softmax_acc(clf(Mv).detach(), Yv)

    # ACC with predicted motifs (indirect path active)
    Mv_pred = enc(Xv).detach()
    acc_indirect = softmax_acc(clf(Mv_pred), Yv)

    # ACC when mediator is ablated (zeros) -- indirect path blocked
    acc_blocked = softmax_acc(clf(torch.zeros_like(Mv)), Yv)

    # Effects
    # Indirect = ACC(predicted mediator) - ACC(blocked mediator)
    # Direct  = ACC(true mediator) - ACC(predicted mediator)  (what expr can't explain via motif)
    indirect_effects.append(float(acc_indirect - acc_blocked))
    direct_effects.append(float(acc_true - acc_indirect))
    accs.append({"acc_true":float(acc_true), "acc_indirect":float(acc_indirect), "acc_blocked":float(acc_blocked)})

print("Nonlinear mediation (mean over folds)")
print("  Indirect effect (Expr→Motif→Role):", np.mean(indirect_effects))
print("  Residual/direct effect (Motif parts not captured by Expr):", np.mean(direct_effects))
print("  Accuracies:", {k: np.mean([a[k] for a in accs]) for k in accs[0]})


# [CELL] 
# ==== SPATIAL EMBEDDINGS via Laplacian Eigenmaps + EGNN-lite ====
import networkx as nx, numpy as np, torch, torch.nn as nn
from scipy import sparse
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold

# Build symmetric adjacency for Laplacian
A = nx.to_scipy_sparse_array(G.subgraph(neurons), nodelist=neurons, weight='w', dtype=float, format='csr')
A = ((A + A.T) / 2.0).tocsr()
d = np.array(A.sum(1)).ravel()
L = sparse.diags(d) - A
# small eigendecomp (3 coords)
L_reg = L + sparse.eye(L.shape[0])*1e-6
vals, vecs = sparse.linalg.eigsh(L_reg, k=3, which='SM')
coords = vecs.astype(np.float32)  # (N,3)

# EGNN-lite: uses pairwise distances to modulate messages
class EGNNLite(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_x, d_pos=3, hidden=64, steps=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.steps = steps
        self.phi_e = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(d_x*2+1, hidden), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(hidden, hidden))
        self.phi_h = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(d_x+hidden, hidden), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(hidden, d_x))
        self.read = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(d_x,64), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(64,5))
    def forward(self, x, pos, adj):
        h = x
        for _ in range(self.steps):
            # message passing with distances
            # adj is (N,N) binary/weighted torch tensor
            # build messages m_ij = phi_e([h_i,h_j, ||pos_i-pos_j||])
            with torch.no_grad():
                D = torch.cdist(pos, pos)  # (N,N)
            hi = h.unsqueeze(1).repeat(1,h.size(0),1)
            hj = h.unsqueeze(0).repeat(h.size(0),1,1)
            m_in = torch.cat([hi, hj, D.unsqueeze(-1)], dim=-1)  # (N,N,2d+1)
            m = self.phi_e(m_in) * adj.unsqueeze(-1)             # mask by adjacency
            m = m.sum(1)                                         # aggregate
            h = h + self.phi_h(torch.cat([h,m], dim=-1))         # update
        return self.read(h)

# Inputs: expression PCs + degree (small, fast)
Zx = PCA(n_components=32, random_state=0).fit_transform((expr-expr.mean())/(expr.std()+1e-6)).astype(np.float32)
deg_small = deg_df.reindex(neurons).values.astype(np.float32)
Xgeo = np.hstack([Zx, deg_small]).astype(np.float32)
Adj = (A>0).astype(np.float32).toarray()

kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=1337)
r_scores=[]
for tr,va in kf.split(Xgeo):
    model = EGNNLite(d_x=Xgeo.shape[1], steps=3).float()
    opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=2e-3)
    crit = nn.MSELoss()
    xtr = torch.tensor(Xgeo[tr]); xva = torch.tensor(Xgeo[va])
    ptr = torch.tensor(coords[tr]); pva = torch.tensor(coords[va])
    atr = torch.tensor(Adj[np.ix_(tr,tr)]); ava = torch.tensor(Adj[np.ix_(va,va)])
    ytr = torch.tensor(PCA(n_components=5, random_state=0).fit_transform(motifs[tr]).astype(np.float32))
    # Fit
    for ep in range(120):
        opt.zero_grad()
        loss = crit(model(xtr, ptr, atr), ytr); loss.backward(); opt.step()
    # Eval correlation on PCs (approx)
    with torch.no_grad():
        pv = model(xva, pva, ava).numpy()
    yv = PCA(n_components=5, random_state=0).fit_transform(motifs[va]).astype(np.float32)
    r = np.corrcoef(yv.flatten(), pv.flatten())[0,1]
    r_scores.append(float(r))
print("EGNN-lite (geometry-aware) motif PC correlation:", np.mean(r_scores))


# [CELL] 
# ==== CONDITIONAL DIFFUSION: p(Motif | Expr PCs) ====
import torch, torch.nn as nn, numpy as np
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

# Data
Zx = PCA(n_components=32, random_state=0).fit_transform((expr-expr.mean())/(expr.std()+1e-6)).astype(np.float32)
Ym = motifs.astype(np.float32)

Xtr, Xva, Ytr, Yva = train_test_split(Zx, Ym, test_size=0.2, random_state=42)

# Simple T-step DDPM setup
T = 100
betas = torch.linspace(1e-4, 0.02, T).float()
alphas = 1.0 - betas
alphas_cum = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)

class CondEpsNet(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_y=5, d_x=32):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(d_y + d_x + 1, 128), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(128,128), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(128, d_y)
        )
    def forward(self, y_noisy, x, t):
        tnorm = (t.float()/T).unsqueeze(-1)
        return self.net(torch.cat([y_noisy, x, tnorm], dim=1))

def q_sample(y0, t, noise):
    ac = alphas_cum[t].unsqueeze(-1)
    return torch.sqrt(ac)*y0 + torch.sqrt(1.0 - ac)*noise

net = CondEpsNet()
opt = torch.optim.Adam(net.parameters(), lr=2e-3)
Xtr_t = torch.tensor(Xtr); Ytr_t = torch.tensor(Ytr)

# Train
for step in range(4000):
    idx = torch.randint(0, Xtr_t.size(0), (64,))
    x = Xtr_t[idx]
    y0 = Ytr_t[idx]
    t = torch.randint(0, T, (x.size(0),))
    noise = torch.randn_like(y0)
    y_noisy = q_sample(y0, t, noise)
    pred = net(y_noisy, x, t)
    loss = nn.MSELoss()(pred, noise)
    opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); opt.step()
    if (step+1)%800==0:
        print("step", step+1, "loss", float(loss.detach()))

# Sample motifs given expression (validation set)
def p_sample_loop(x_cond, n_samples=1):
    net.eval()
    y = torch.randn((x_cond.size(0), Ym.shape[1]))
    for t in reversed(range(T)):
        tt = torch.full((x_cond.size(0),), t)
        eps = net(y, x_cond, tt)
        a = alphas[t]; ac = alphas_cum[t]
        y = (1/torch.sqrt(a))*(y - (betas[t]/torch.sqrt(1-ac))*eps)
        if t>0:
            y += torch.sqrt(betas[t])*torch.randn_like(y)
    return y

with torch.no_grad():
    Xv_t = torch.tensor(Xva)
    y_gen = p_sample_loop(Xv_t)
    # Compare generated to true via correlation
    corr = np.corrcoef(y_gen.numpy().flatten(), Yva.flatten())[0,1]
    print("Conditional diffusion motif correlation (val):", float(corr))


# [CELL] 
# ==== STABILITY SELECTION for gene -> motif PCs ====
import numpy as np, pandas as pd
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.linear_model import Lasso
from sklearn.utils import resample

# Reduce gene space (variance screen), then stability selection
gene_var = expr.var(0).sort_values(ascending=False)
top_genes = list(gene_var.head(2000).index)  # adjust if needed
Xe = ((expr[top_genes]-expr[top_genes].mean())/(expr[top_genes].std()+1e-6)).values.astype(np.float32)

mpcs = PCA(n_components=3, random_state=0).fit_transform(motifs).astype(np.float32)

def stability_select(X, y, n_boot=100, alpha=0.005):
    freq = np.zeros(X.shape[1], dtype=float)
    for b in range(n_boot):
        Xb, yb = resample(X, y, n_samples=int(0.7*X.shape[0]), replace=True, random_state=1000+b)
        model = Lasso(alpha=alpha, max_iter=4000).fit(Xb, yb)
        freq += (model.coef_!=0).astype(float)
    return freq / n_boot

results = {}
for k in range(mpcs.shape[1]):
    sel = stability_select(Xe, mpcs[:,k], n_boot=120, alpha=0.003)
    s = pd.Series(sel, index=top_genes).sort_values(ascending=False)
    results[f"motif_pc{k+1}"] = s
    print(f"Top genes for motif_pc{k+1}:")
    display(s.head(20))

# Save combined table of selection frequencies (if you want to persist)
stable_tbl = pd.concat(results, axis=1)
stable_tbl.head(10)

import pandas as pd, requests, io

# Download latest mapping table
url = "https://ftp.wormbase.org/pub/wormbase/species/c_elegans/annotation/geneIDs.txt"
mapping = pd.read_csv(url, sep="\t", header=None, names=["WBGeneID","SeqName","GeneName"])

# clean nulls
mapping = mapping.dropna(subset=["WBGeneID"])
print("Mapping loaded:", mapping.shape)
display(mapping.head())

# merge with your stability table
mapped = stable_tbl.copy()
mapped.index.name = "WBGeneID"
mapped = mapped.reset_index().merge(mapping[["WBGeneID","GeneName"]], on="WBGeneID", how="left")
mapped = mapped.set_index("GeneName").drop(columns=["WBGeneID"])

display(mapped.head(20))
