# [CELL] 
import networkx as nx
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from collections import Counter
from networkx.algorithms import approximation as approx
from networkx.generators.random_graphs import fast_gnp_random_graph
import seaborn as sns

# 🔄 Convert edge list to directed graph
G = nx.from_pandas_edgelist(
    edges_df,
    source="Source",
    target="Target",
    edge_attr="SynapseCount",
    create_using=nx.DiGraph()
)

# 📊 Basic stats
print(f"🧠 Nodes: {G.number_of_nodes()}")
print(f"🧠 Edges: {G.number_of_edges()}")
print(f"🔁 Self-loops: {nx.number_of_selfloops(G)}")
print(f"⚖️ Density: {nx.density(G):.5f}")
print(f"🔗 Is strongly connected? {nx.is_strongly_connected(G)}")

# 🌐 Component analysis
weak_components = list(nx.weakly_connected_components(G))
component_sizes = [len(c) for c in weak_components]
print(f"🧩 Number of weakly connected components: {len(weak_components)}")
print(f"🧩 Largest component size: {max(component_sizes)}")

# 🧮 Degree statistics
degrees = dict(G.degree(weight=None))
strengths = dict(G.degree(weight="SynapseCount"))
avg_degree = np.mean(list(degrees.values()))
avg_strength = np.mean(list(strengths.values()))
print(f"📉 Avg Degree: {avg_degree:.2f}")
print(f"📉 Avg Strength: {avg_strength:.2f}")

# 🧪 Rich club (top 10 strongest neurons)
rich_df = pd.DataFrame.from_dict(strengths, orient="index", columns=["TotalSynapseCount"])
rich_df = rich_df.sort_values("TotalSynapseCount", ascending=False)
top10 = rich_df.head(10)
print("\n👑 Top 10 Rich Club Neurons:")
display(top10)

# 🎲 Null Model 1: Erdős–Rényi
N, E = G.number_of_nodes(), G.number_of_edges()
p = E / (N * (N - 1))  # directed version
G_er = fast_gnp_random_graph(N, p, directed=True)

# 🎲 Null Model 2: Degree-Preserving Configuration Model
G_config = nx.directed_configuration_model(
    [G.out_degree(n) for n in G.nodes()],
    [G.in_degree(n) for n in G.nodes()],
    create_using=nx.DiGraph()
)
G_config = nx.DiGraph(G_config)  # Remove parallel edges
G_config.remove_edges_from(nx.selfloop_edges(G_config))  # Remove self-loops

# 📈 Compare average path length and clustering
def safe_avg_path_length(graph):
    if nx.is_strongly_connected(graph):
        return nx.average_shortest_path_length(graph)
    else:
        # Get largest weakly connected component
        largest_weak_cc = max(nx.weakly_connected_components(graph), key=len)
        subgraph = graph.subgraph(largest_weak_cc).copy()
        
        # Extract largest strongly connected subcomponent inside it
        strongly_connected_subs = list(nx.strongly_connected_components(subgraph))
        if strongly_connected_subs:
            largest_strong_cc = max(strongly_connected_subs, key=len)
            strong_subgraph = subgraph.subgraph(largest_strong_cc)
            return nx.average_shortest_path_length(strong_subgraph)
        else:
            return np.nan

def safe_avg_clustering(graph):
    try:
        return approx.average_clustering(graph)
    except Exception:
        return np.nan

print("\n🌍 Global Metrics Comparison:")
print(f"Real - Avg Path Length: {safe_avg_path_length(G):.2f}")
print(f"ER   - Avg Path Length: {safe_avg_path_length(G_er):.2f}")
print(f"Conf - Avg Path Length: {safe_avg_path_length(G_config):.2f}")

print(f"Real - Clustering Coef: {safe_avg_clustering(G):.4f}")
print(f"ER   - Clustering Coef: {safe_avg_clustering(G_er):.4f}")
print(f"Conf - Clustering Coef: {safe_avg_clustering(G_config):.4f}")


# [CELL] 
# ---------------------------- SECTION 1 ----------------------------
# 🧬 Imports and Environment Setup
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import networkx as nx
from collections import Counter
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from networkx.algorithms import approximation as approx
from networkx.generators.random_graphs import fast_gnp_random_graph

sns.set(style="whitegrid")
pd.set_option("display.max_columns", None)

EDGE_FILE    = "data/connectivity/processed/merged_edges_with_relatedness.csv"
LINEAGE_FILE = "data/lineage/processed/merged_lineage_table.csv"

print("✅ Libraries imported; data paths set.")


# [CELL] 
# ---------------------------- SECTION 2 ----------------------------
# 🧠 Load Core Data
edges_df   = pd.read_csv(EDGE_FILE)
lineage_df = pd.read_csv(LINEAGE_FILE)

print("📦 edges_df :", edges_df.shape)
print("📦 lineage_df:", lineage_df.shape)

# Basic schema assertions
assert {"Source", "Target", "Type", "SynapseCount"}.issubset(edges_df.columns)
assert {"Neuron1", "Relatedness"}.issubset(lineage_df.columns)

display(edges_df.head(3))
display(lineage_df.head(3))


# [CELL] 
# ---------------------------- SECTION 3 ----------------------------
# 🔍 Motif Counting (triads) & Rich Score
G = nx.from_pandas_edgelist(
    edges_df, source="Source", target="Target", edge_attr="SynapseCount", create_using=nx.DiGraph()
)

# Pre-allocate motif counts per neuron
motif_cols = [f"Motif{i}" for i in range(10)]
motif_counts = pd.DataFrame(0, index=G.nodes(), columns=motif_cols)

# VERY-fast triad census (directed)
triad_dict = nx.triadic_census(G)
for triad_code, count in triad_dict.items():
    idx = int(triad_code[-1])   # maps '030T' etc. to integer bucket 0-9
    motif_counts[f"Motif{idx}"] += count

motif_counts["RichScore"] = motif_counts.sum(axis=1)
print("✅ Motif counting complete; RichScore computed.")
display(motif_counts.head())


# [CELL] 
# ---------------------------- SECTION 4 ----------------------------
# 🧠 Symbolic Role Classification (KMeans)
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from scipy.stats import chi2_contingency

X = StandardScaler().fit_transform(motif_counts[motif_cols])
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=5, random_state=42).fit(X)
motif_counts["Cluster"] = kmeans.labels_

# Heuristic label mapping
cluster_to_role = {
    0: "Oscillator 🔁",
    1: "Integrator 🧩",
    2: "Relay ➡️",
    3: "Diverger 🌳",
    4: "Unclassified ❓"
}
motif_counts["SymbolicRole"] = motif_counts["Cluster"].map(cluster_to_role)

# Cramér’s V for role × cluster
crosstab = pd.crosstab(motif_counts["SymbolicRole"], motif_counts["Cluster"])
chi2, p, dof, _ = chi2_contingency(crosstab)
cramers_v = np.sqrt(chi2 / (crosstab.sum().sum() * (min(crosstab.shape) - 1)))

print(f"📐 Cramér’s V (Role vs Cluster): {cramers_v:.3f}")
display(crosstab)


# [CELL] 
# ---------------------------- SECTION 5 ----------------------------
# 📊 Structural Enrichment Attempt (may be skipped if index mismatch)
motif_df = lineage_df[["Neuron1", "Relatedness"]].drop_duplicates("Neuron1").set_index("Neuron1")
merged = motif_counts.join(motif_df, how="left")

if merged["Relatedness"].isna().all():
    print("⚠️ No overlap between motif_counts and motif_df; structural enrichment deferred.")
else:
    print("✅ Structural columns merged.")
    display(merged.head())


# [CELL] 
# ---------------------------- SECTION 6 ----------------------------
# 👑 Rich-Club Neurons by Weighted Degree
strengths = dict(G.degree(weight="SynapseCount"))
rich_df = (pd.Series(strengths, name="TotalSynapseCount")
           .sort_values(ascending=False).to_frame())
top10 = rich_df.head(10)
print("👑 Top 10 Rich-Club Neurons:")
display(top10)
motif_counts["TotalSynapseCount"] = pd.Series(strengths)


# [CELL] 
# ---------------------------- SECTION 7 ----------------------------
# 🔁 Global Graph Metrics & Null Models
def safe_avg_path_length(graph):
    if nx.is_strongly_connected(graph):
        return nx.average_shortest_path_length(graph)
    largest_weak = max(nx.weakly_connected_components(graph), key=len)
    sub = graph.subgraph(largest_weak)
    largest_strong = max(nx.strongly_connected_components(sub), key=len)
    return nx.average_shortest_path_length(sub.subgraph(largest_strong))

def safe_clustering(graph):
    try:
        return approx.average_clustering(graph)
    except:
        return np.nan

print(f"Nodes: {G.number_of_nodes()} | Edges: {G.number_of_edges()}")
print(f"Density: {nx.density(G):.5f}")

# Null graphs
N, E = G.number_of_nodes(), G.number_of_edges()
p = E / (N * (N - 1))
G_er = fast_gnp_random_graph(N, p, directed=True)
G_cfg = nx.directed_configuration_model(
    [G.out_degree(n) for n in G], [G.in_degree(n) for n in G], create_using=nx.DiGraph()
)
G_cfg = nx.DiGraph(G_cfg); G_cfg.remove_edges_from(nx.selfloop_edges(G_cfg))

print("\n🌍 Average Path Length:")
print("Real :", f"{safe_avg_path_length(G):.2f}")
print("ER   :", f"{safe_avg_path_length(G_er):.2f}")
print("CFG  :", f"{safe_avg_path_length(G_cfg):.2f}")

print("\n🌍 Clustering Coefficient:")
print("Real :", f"{safe_clustering(G):.4f}")
print("ER   :", f"{safe_clustering(G_er):.4f}")
print("CFG  :", f"{safe_clustering(G_cfg):.4f}")


# [CELL] 
# ---------------------------- SECTION 8 ----------------------------
# 🧠 Exploratory Plots (Synapse Distribution, Type Breakdown, Relatedness)
sns.histplot(edges_df["SynapseCount"], bins=50, log_scale=(False, True), color="cornflowerblue")
plt.title("Synapse Count Distribution (log-Y)")
plt.show()

type_summary = edges_df.groupby("Type")["SynapseCount"].agg(['count', 'sum'])
type_summary["sum"].plot.bar(color="salmon")
plt.title("Total Synaptic Strength by Type")
plt.ylabel("Synapse Count")
plt.show()

if "Relatedness" in lineage_df.columns:
    sns.histplot(lineage_df["Relatedness"], bins=20, color="orchid")
    plt.title("Lineage Relatedness Distribution")
    plt.show()


# [CELL] 
# ---------------------------- SECTION 9 ----------------------------
# 🧾 Final Summary & Export
summary = {
    "Total Neurons": G.number_of_nodes(),
    "Total Edges": G.number_of_edges(),
    "Density": nx.density(G),
    "Weakly Connected Components": nx.number_weakly_connected_components(G),
    "Largest Component Size": max(len(c) for c in nx.weakly_connected_components(G))
}
print("📈 Phase 1 Summary:", summary)

# Export motif_counts with symbolic roles & strengths
motif_counts.to_csv("phase1_results.csv")
print("✅ Saved phase1_results.csv")

# Next Phase Note
print("\n🚀 Next Up: Phase 2 – Dynamic Motif Simulation & Functional Overlay")


# [CELL] 
import networkx as nx
from itertools import combinations
from tqdm import tqdm
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# 🔁 Step 1: Load your graph from Phase 1
# Assuming you have G already in memory (directed, weighted)
# If not, load from CSV:
# edges_df = pd.read_csv("merged_edges_with_relatedness.csv")
# G = nx.from_pandas_edgelist(edges_df, source="Source", target="Target", edge_attr=True, create_using=nx.DiGraph)

# 🧠 Step 2: Enumerate all 3-node subgraphs
motif_counts = Counter()
node_motif_participation = defaultdict(lambda: Counter())

print("🔍 Enumerating all 3-node directed subgraphs...")
nodes = list(G.nodes())
for triplet in tqdm(combinations(nodes, 3), total=len(nodes)**3 // 6):
    subgraph = G.subgraph(triplet).copy()
    if not nx.is_weakly_connected(subgraph): continue
    # Relabel nodes for canonical form
    canon = nx.convert_node_labels_to_integers(subgraph)
    gm = nx.algorithms.isomorphism.GraphMatcher(canon, canon)
    # Use canonical edge tuple list as signature
    edge_signature = tuple(sorted(canon.edges()))
    motif_counts[edge_signature] += 1
    for node in subgraph.nodes():
        node_motif_participation[node][edge_signature] += 1

# 🧪 Step 3: Convert to dataframe
motif_df = pd.DataFrame.from_dict(node_motif_participation, orient="index").fillna(0)
motif_df.index.name = "Neuron"

# 💾 Step 4: Save results
motif_df.to_csv("motif_participation_profiles.csv")
pd.Series(motif_counts).to_csv("motif_global_counts.csv")

print("✅ Motif enumeration and neuron projection complete.")


# [CELL] 
# Check index type
print("Index type:", type(motif_df.index))
print("First few index values:", motif_df.index[:5])

# If the index is not plain neuron strings, flatten it
if isinstance(motif_df.index[0], (tuple, list)):
    motif_df.index = motif_df.index.map(lambda x: "_".join(map(str, x)))
    print("✅ Flattened complex index for CSV export.")

# Retry saving
motif_df.to_csv("motif_participation_profiles.csv")
pd.Series(motif_counts).to_csv("motif_global_counts.csv")

print("✅ Motif outputs successfully saved.")


# [CELL] 
# 🔍 Check and flatten index if it's a MultiIndex or has sequences
if isinstance(motif_df.index, pd.MultiIndex) or isinstance(motif_df.index[0], (tuple, list)):
    motif_df.index = motif_df.index.map(lambda x: "_".join(map(str, x)) if isinstance(x, (tuple, list)) else str(x))
    motif_df.index.name = "Neuron"
    print("✅ Flattened complex MultiIndex into neuron name strings.")

# 🔍 Check for any remaining multi-level columns
if isinstance(motif_df.columns, pd.MultiIndex):
    motif_df.columns = ["_".join(map(str, col)) if isinstance(col, tuple) else str(col) for col in motif_df.columns]
    print("✅ Flattened column MultiIndex.")

# 💾 Retry saving to CSV
motif_df.to_csv("motif_participation_profiles.csv")
pd.Series(motif_counts).to_csv("motif_global_counts.csv")

print("✅ Motif outputs successfully saved to CSV.")


# [CELL]

motif_counts_raw = pd.read_csv("motif_global_counts.csv", header=None)
print(motif_counts_raw.shape)

# [CELL] 
import networkx as nx
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# Load cleaned motif counts
motif_counts = pd.read_csv("motif_global_counts_cleaned.csv", index_col=0)

# Sort motifs by count
motif_counts_sorted = motif_counts.sort_values("Count", ascending=False)

# Function to draw a motif from a string like '(2, 0)+(2, 1)'
def draw_motif(motif_str, count, ax=None):
    edges = motif_str.split("+")
    edges = [eval(edge) for edge in edges]  # Convert to tuples

    G = nx.DiGraph()
    G.add_edges_from(edges)

    # Fixed 3-node layout for consistency
    pos = {0: (0, 1), 1: (1, 1), 2: (0.5, 0)}
    if ax is None:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots()

    nx.draw(G, pos, with_labels=True, arrows=True, node_size=800, node_color='lightblue', ax=ax)
    ax.set_title(f"{motif_str}\nCount: {count}")
    ax.axis('off')

# Plot top K
K = 10
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, K // 2, figsize=(18, 6))

for i, (motif_str, row) in enumerate(motif_counts_sorted.head(K).iterrows()):
    ax = axes[i // (K // 2)][i % (K // 2)]
    draw_motif(motif_str, row["Count"], ax=ax)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()


# [CELL] 
def classify_motif(motif_str):
    edges = motif_str.split("+")
    edge_set = set(tuple(map(int, e.strip("()").split(","))) for e in edges)

    # Count edges from each source node
    from collections import Counter
    out_degree = Counter([src for src, tgt in edge_set])
    in_degree = Counter([tgt for src, tgt in edge_set])

    if any(src == tgt for src, tgt in edge_set):
        if len(edge_set) == 1:
            return "Self-loop"
        elif len(edge_set) == 2:
            return "Self-loop + Outbound"
    elif any((a, b) in edge_set and (b, a) in edge_set for a, b in edge_set):
        return "Reciprocal / 2-cycle"
    elif len(out_degree) == 1 and list(out_degree.values())[0] == 2:
        return "Fan-out"
    elif len(in_degree) == 1 and list(in_degree.values())[0] == 2:
        return "Fan-in"
    elif len(edge_set) == 2:
        return "Mixed"
    else:
        return "Other"


# [CELL] 
import pandas as pd

# Load cleaned counts
motif_counts = pd.read_csv("motif_global_counts_cleaned.csv", index_col=0)

# Define classification logic
def classify_motif(motif_str):
    try:
        edges = motif_str.split("+")
        edge_set = set(tuple(map(int, e.strip("()").split(","))) for e in edges)

        from collections import Counter
        out_degree = Counter([src for src, tgt in edge_set])
        in_degree = Counter([tgt for src, tgt in edge_set])

        if any(src == tgt for src, tgt in edge_set):
            if len(edge_set) == 1:
                return "Self-loop"
            elif len(edge_set) == 2:
                return "Self-loop + Outbound"
        elif any((a, b) in edge_set and (b, a) in edge_set for a, b in edge_set):
            return "Reciprocal / 2-cycle"
        elif len(out_degree) == 1 and list(out_degree.values())[0] == 2:
            return "Fan-out"
        elif len(in_degree) == 1 and list(in_degree.values())[0] == 2:
            return "Fan-in"
        elif len(edge_set) == 2:
            return "Mixed"
        else:
            return "Other"
    except:
        return "ParseError"

# Apply classification
motif_counts["Type"] = motif_counts.index.map(classify_motif)

# Save labeled version
motif_counts.to_csv("motif_global_counts_labeled.csv")
print("✅ Saved labeled CSV as `motif_global_counts_labeled.csv`.")


# [CELL] 
import pandas as pd

# Load labeled motif data
motif_df = pd.read_csv("motif_global_counts_labeled.csv", index_col=0)

# Sort by frequency
top_motifs = motif_df.sort_values("Count", ascending=False).head(15)

# Reset index for display
top_motifs = top_motifs.reset_index().rename(columns={"index": "Motif"})

# Add rank
top_motifs.insert(0, "Rank", range(1, len(top_motifs) + 1))

# Convert to LaTeX
latex_code = top_motifs.to_latex(index=False, column_format="rlrr", escape=False)

# Save to .tex file
with open("top_motifs_table.tex", "w") as f:
    f.write(latex_code)

print("✅ Saved LaTeX table as `top_motifs_table.tex`.")


# [CELL] 
import os
import ast
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import networkx as nx
import pandas as pd

# Load top motifs
df = pd.read_csv("motif_global_counts_labeled.csv", index_col=0)
df_sorted = df.sort_values("Count", ascending=False).head(15).reset_index()

# Create output folder
output_dir = "motif_graphs"
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

# Function to draw and save motif graph
def draw_motif(motif_id, edges, count, index):
    G = nx.DiGraph()
    for edge in edges:
        G.add_edge(*edge)
    
    plt.figure(figsize=(2.5, 2.5))
    pos = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42)
    nx.draw(G, pos, with_labels=True, node_size=800, node_color="skyblue", arrows=True)
    plt.title(f"#{index+1}: {motif_id}\nCount: {count}", fontsize=10)
    plt.tight_layout()
    
    filename = f"motif_{index+1}_{motif_id.replace(' ', '').replace('(','').replace(')','').replace(',','_').replace('+','-')}.svg"
    filepath = os.path.join(output_dir, filename)
    plt.savefig(filepath, format="svg")
    plt.close()
    return filepath

# Generate graphs
print("🧠 Generating motif diagrams...")
for i, row in df_sorted.iterrows():
    motif_id = row['MotifID']
    count = row['Count']
    
    # Parse edges
    try:
        edges = [ast.literal_eval(e.strip()) for e in motif_id.split("+")]
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"⚠️ Failed to parse {motif_id}: {e}")
        continue
    
    path = draw_motif(motif_id, edges, count, i)
    print(f"✅ Saved: {path}")


# [CELL] 
import ast

def safe_parse_motif_id(motif_str):
    try:
        return [ast.literal_eval(e.strip()) for e in motif_str.split("+")]
    except Exception:
        return None  # Malformed motif

# Apply safely
motif_counts["Edges"] = motif_counts.index.map(safe_parse_motif_id)

# Drop invalid motifs (e.g. NaN or malformed)
motif_counts = motif_counts.dropna(subset=["Edges"])

# Now classify
def classify_motif(edges):
    edges_set = set(edges)
    nodes = {n for edge in edges for n in edge}
    E = {(a, b): True for a, b in edges}

    if len(edges) == 0:
        return "Isolated"
    elif len(edges) == 1:
        return "Single Edge"
    elif len(edges) == 2:
        if (0, 1) in E and (1, 0) in E or (1, 2) in E and (2, 1) in E:
            return "Mutual Dyad"
        elif (0, 1) in E and (0, 2) in E:
            return "Star"
        elif (0, 1) in E and (1, 2) in E:
            return "Chain"
        else:
            return "Other 2-edge"
    elif len(edges) == 3:
        if (0, 1) in E and (1, 2) in E and (0, 2) in E:
            return "Feedforward Loop"
        elif (0, 1) in E and (1, 0) in E:
            return "Feedback Loop"
        else:
            return "Other 3-edge"
    else:
        return "Other"

motif_counts["Class"] = motif_counts["Edges"].map(classify_motif)
motif_counts = motif_counts[["Count", "Edges", "Class"]]
motif_counts.to_csv("motif_class_table.csv")
print("✅ Saved: motif_class_table.csv")


# [CELL] 
import pandas as pd

# === Step 1: Load motif participation and class tables ===

# 🧬 Per-neuron motif participation
motif_df = pd.read_csv("motif_participation_profiles.csv", index_col=0)

# 🧾 Motif class labels
motif_classes = pd.read_csv("motif_class_table.csv", index_col=0)

# === Step 2: Join motif counts with class labels ===

# Transpose so motifs are rows, neurons are columns
motif_df_T = motif_df.T

# Add class labels to motifs
motif_df_T["Class"] = motif_df_T.index.map(motif_classes["Class"])

# === Step 3: Group by class and sum counts ===

# Group all motifs by class and sum across motifs
class_profiles = motif_df_T.groupby("Class").sum().T

# === Step 4: Output and preview ===

# Save to disk
class_profiles.to_csv("neuron_motif_roles.csv")

# Preview
print("✅ Saved neuron → motif role projection to `neuron_motif_roles.csv`")
display(class_profiles.head())


# [CELL] 
import pandas as pd

# Motif participation profiles: Neuron x MotifID
motif_df = pd.read_csv("motif_participation_profiles.csv", index_col=0)

# MotifID → Class mapping
motif_classes = pd.read_csv("motif_class_table.csv", index_col=0)


# [CELL] 
# Clean column MultiIndex or weird stringified columns
motif_df.columns = motif_df.columns.astype(str)

# Rename motif columns based on class
col_map = {}
for col in motif_df.columns:
    if col in motif_classes.index:
        motif_class = motif_classes.loc[col, "Class"]
        col_map[col] = motif_class
    else:
        col_map[col] = "Unclassified ❓"

# Apply mapping
motif_df = motif_df.rename(columns=col_map)


# [CELL] 
# Sum across duplicated class columns
motif_role_matrix = motif_df.groupby(level=0, axis=1).sum()

# Save clean result
motif_role_matrix.index.name = "Neuron"
motif_role_matrix.to_csv("neuron_motif_roles.csv")

print("✅ Regenerated and saved: neuron_motif_roles.csv")
display(motif_role_matrix.head())

# [CELL] 
import pandas as pd

# Load motif role matrix
motif_roles = pd.read_csv("neuron_motif_roles.csv", index_col=0)

# Load metadata
metadata = pd.read_csv("neuron_metadata.csv", index_col=0)  # Should include a 'Type' column

# Merge
merged = motif_roles.merge(metadata, left_index=True, right_index=True)

# Check
print(merged.head())


# [CELL] 
import scipy.stats as stats

# Store results
results = []

# Loop through each motif class
for col in motif_roles.columns:
    try:
        # Group by neuron type
        groups = [group[col].dropna().values for name, group in merged.groupby("Type")]

        # One-way ANOVA
        f_stat, p_val = stats.f_oneway(*groups)

        results.append({"MotifClass": col, "F-statistic": f_stat, "p-value": p_val})
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error with {col}: {e}")

# Convert to DataFrame
anova_df = pd.DataFrame(results).sort_values("p-value")

# Save results
anova_df.to_csv("motif_type_anova_results.csv", index=False)
print(anova_df.head())


# [CELL] 
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Pick top 5 motifs with lowest p-value
top_motifs = anova_df.nsmallest(5, "p-value")["MotifClass"].values

# Melt the DataFrame for seaborn boxplot
plot_df = merged[top_motifs.tolist() + ["Type"]].melt(id_vars="Type", var_name="MotifClass", value_name="Count")

# Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.boxplot(data=plot_df, x="MotifClass", y="Count", hue="Type")
plt.title("Top Motif Roles Differentiating Neuron Types")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("motif_type_boxplot.png")
plt.show()


# [CELL] 
import networkx as nx
import ast

def classify_motif_structure(motif_id):
    try:
        edges = [ast.literal_eval(e.strip()) for e in motif_id.split("+")]
    except Exception as e:
        return "Malformed"

    G = nx.DiGraph()
    G.add_edges_from(edges)

    # Get basic properties
    num_nodes = G.number_of_nodes()
    num_edges = G.number_of_edges()
    in_degrees = dict(G.in_degree())
    out_degrees = dict(G.out_degree())
    has_cycle = any(nx.simple_cycles(G))
    
    # Heuristics
    if num_edges == 1:
        return "Single edge"
    if has_cycle:
        return "Cycle"
    if all(d == 1 for d in out_degrees.values()) and all(d == 1 for d in in_degrees.values()):
        return "Chain"
    if max(in_degrees.values()) >= 2 and max(out_degrees.values()) == 1:
        return "Fan-in"
    if max(out_degrees.values()) >= 2 and max(in_degrees.values()) == 1:
        return "Fan-out"
    if any(G.has_edge(v, u) for u, v in edges):  # bidirectional
        return "Bidirectional"
    if num_edges == 2 and num_nodes == 2:
        return "Reciprocal pair"
    
    return "Other"

# Apply to motif count table
motif_counts["Structure"] = motif_counts.index.map(classify_motif_structure)

# Save
motif_counts.to_csv("motif_counts_with_structure.csv")


# [CELL] 
# 🔍 CELL 1: Recursively find all CSV/XLS/XLSX files and inspect structure
import os
import pandas as pd

def scan_dataset_files(base_path="."):
    valid_extensions = [".csv", ".xls", ".xlsx"]
    dataset_info = []

    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(base_path):
        for file in files:
            ext = os.path.splitext(file)[1].lower()
            if ext in valid_extensions:
                full_path = os.path.join(root, file)
                try:
                    if ext == ".csv":
                        df = pd.read_csv(full_path, nrows=100)
                    else:
                        df = pd.read_excel(full_path, nrows=100)
                    dataset_info.append({
                        "File": full_path,
                        "Shape": df.shape,
                        "Columns": df.columns.tolist()
                    })
                except Exception as e:
                    dataset_info.append({
                        "File": full_path,
                        "Error": str(e)
                    })

    return dataset_info

# Run the scan and print results
dataset_summaries = scan_dataset_files(".")
for entry in dataset_summaries:
    print("📁 File:", entry["File"])
    if "Error" in entry:
        print("   ❌ Error:", entry["Error"])
    else:
        print("   📐 Shape:", entry["Shape"])
        print("   🔑 Columns:", entry["Columns"])
    print("-" * 80)


# [CELL] 
# 🔎 CELL 2: Inspect all in-memory pandas DataFrames
import pandas as pd
import sys

def describe_all_dataframes():
    frame_info = []
    global_vars = globals()

    for var_name, var_value in global_vars.items():
        if isinstance(var_value, pd.DataFrame):
            df = var_value
            frame_info.append({
                "Name": var_name,
                "Shape": df.shape,
                "Columns": df.columns.tolist(),
                "DTypes": df.dtypes.to_dict()
            })

    return frame_info

# Print summaries
dataframe_summaries = describe_all_dataframes()
for entry in dataframe_summaries:
    print(f"📊 DataFrame: {entry['Name']}")
    print(f"   📐 Shape: {entry['Shape']}")
    print(f"   🔑 Columns: {entry['Columns']}")
    print(f"   🧬 DTypes: {entry['DTypes']}")
    print("-" * 80)


# [CELL] 
print("🧬 lineage_df columns:", lineage_df.columns.tolist())
print("📐 lineage_df shape:", lineage_df.shape)
print("🧠 lineage_df head:\n", lineage_df.head())


# [CELL] 
# 🔁 Option 2: Compute per-neuron lineage stats
lineage_long = pd.concat([
    lineage_df.rename(columns={"NeuronA": "Neuron", "NeuronB": "Other"}),
    lineage_df.rename(columns={"NeuronB": "Neuron", "NeuronA": "Other"})
])

lineage_stats = lineage_long.groupby("Neuron")["Relatedness"].agg(
    LineageMean="mean",
    LineageMin="min",
    LineageMax="max"
).reset_index()

# Merge into master_df
master_df = master_df.merge(lineage_stats, how="left", on="Neuron")

print("✅ Added lineage-related stats to master_df")

neurons_target = set(connectome_df["Target"])

canonical_df = pd.DataFrame({"Neuron": canonical_neurons})
