from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, confusion_matrix
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier, export_text
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# Viz
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from tqdm import tqdm

# Paths & logging
OUT_DIR = "./output/track2"
os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
logging.basicConfig(
    filename=os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "track2.log"),
    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s"
)
print("✅ Track 2 environment ready. Outputs ->", OUT_DIR)

# Repro
SEED = 42
random.seed(SEED)
np.random.seed(SEED)
torch.manual_seed(SEED)


# [CELL] 
CONFIG = {
    "device": "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu",
    "hidden_dim": 128,
    "dropout": 0.25,
    "lr": 2e-3,
    "epochs": 250,
    "explainer_epochs": 200,
    "val_size": 0.2,
    "test_size": 0.2,  # of the remaining after val split
    "min_class_support": 10  # drop ultra-rare labels to stabilize training
}
CONFIG


# [CELL] 
# Files expected in the same directory as notebook
graph_path = "./merged_master_graph.gml"
lineage_path = "./lineage_embeddings.pkl"
motif_path = "./motif_summary.csv"

# Load
G = nx.read_gml(graph_path)
lineage = pd.read_pickle(lineage_path)
motifs = pd.read_csv(motif_path, index_col=0)

# Harmonize index/order
nodes = list(G.nodes())
lineage = lineage.reindex(nodes)
motifs = motifs.reindex(nodes)

# Basic label source
# Prefer node attribute 'Type' if present; else try neuron_metadata.csv if you have it
labels_attr = nx.get_node_attributes(G, "Type")
label_series = pd.Series(labels_attr).reindex(nodes)

# Drop nodes with missing labels
mask_labeled = label_series.notna()
nodes_labeled = [n for n, m in zip(nodes, mask_labeled) if m]
print(f"Nodes total: {len(nodes)} | Labeled: {mask_labeled.sum()} | Unlabeled: {(~mask_labeled).sum()}")

# Build feature matrix
X = pd.concat([lineage, motifs], axis=1).loc[nodes_labeled]
X = X.fillna(0.0)

# Encode labels
label_values = label_series.loc[nodes_labeled].astype(str)
label_map = {lab:i for i, lab in enumerate(sorted(label_values.unique()))}
y = label_values.map(label_map)

print("Classes:", label_map)
print("Feature shape:", X.shape)


# [CELL] 
# Drop ultra-rare classes to stabilize training
counts = y.value_counts()
keep_labels = counts[counts >= CONFIG["min_class_support"]].index
keep_mask = y.isin(keep_labels)

# Filter X and y together; keep the order coupled
X = X.loc[keep_mask]
y = y.loc[keep_mask]

# Standardize features for stability
scaler = StandardScaler()
X_std = scaler.fit_transform(X.values)

print("Kept classes & counts:\n", y.value_counts())
print("Final feature shape:", X_std.shape)


# [CELL] 
# Base index after filtering
base_index = X.index.tolist()

# Induce subgraph on the filtered nodes, preserving this order
G_sub = G.subgraph(base_index).copy()

# IMPORTANT: align features and labels to the exact node order used by G_sub
order = list(G_sub.nodes())           # this is the order PyG will use
feat_df = pd.DataFrame(X_std, index=X.index).loc[order]
y_aligned = y.loc[order]

print(f"G_sub: {G_sub.number_of_nodes()} nodes, {G_sub.number_of_edges()} edges")
print("feat_df shape:", feat_df.shape, "| y_aligned len:", len(y_aligned))


# [CELL] 
from torch_geometric.utils import from_networkx

def strip_attributes(Gin: nx.Graph) -> nx.Graph:
    # Preserve directionality
    H = nx.DiGraph() if Gin.is_directed() else nx.Graph()
    H.add_nodes_from(Gin.nodes())              # no node attrs
    H.add_edges_from(Gin.edges())              # no edge attrs
    return H

# Use the already-built G_sub, feat_df, y_aligned
G_clean = strip_attributes(G_sub)

# Convert to PyG (now no heterogeneous attrs to trigger errors)
pyg_data = from_networkx(G_clean)

# Attach our tensors
pyg_data.x = torch.tensor(feat_df.values, dtype=torch.float)
pyg_data.y = torch.tensor(y_aligned.values, dtype=torch.long)

print("Converted to PyG:")
print(pyg_data)


# [CELL] 
# Train/val/test split on THIS aligned order
idx_all = np.arange(len(y_aligned))
idx_train, idx_temp, y_train, y_temp = train_test_split(
    idx_all, y_aligned.values,
    test_size=CONFIG["val_size"] + CONFIG["test_size"],
    stratify=y_aligned.values,
    random_state=SEED
)
rel_test = CONFIG["test_size"] / (CONFIG["val_size"] + CONFIG["test_size"])
idx_val, idx_test, y_val, y_test = train_test_split(
    idx_temp, y_temp,
    test_size=rel_test,
    stratify=y_temp,
    random_state=SEED
)

# Boolean masks
train_mask = torch.zeros(len(y_aligned), dtype=torch.bool)
val_mask   = torch.zeros(len(y_aligned), dtype=torch.bool)
test_mask  = torch.zeros(len(y_aligned), dtype=torch.bool)
train_mask[idx_train] = True
val_mask[idx_val]     = True
test_mask[idx_test]   = True

pyg_data.train_mask = train_mask
pyg_data.val_mask   = val_mask
pyg_data.test_mask  = test_mask

# Move to device
pyg_data = pyg_data.to(CONFIG["device"])
print(pyg_data)


# [CELL] 
UNKNOWN_ID = 3  # adjust if different
mask_known = y_aligned != UNKNOWN_ID
G_sub_known = G_sub.subgraph(pd.Index(y_aligned.index)[mask_known]).copy()

# Rebuild feat_df/y_aligned for known-only, then re-run the two cells above.
feat_df = feat_df.loc[mask_known]
y_aligned = y_aligned.loc[mask_known]


# [CELL] 
class NeurosymbolicGNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_dim, hidden, num_classes, dropout=0.25):
        super().__init__()
        self.gat1 = pyg_nn.GATConv(in_dim, hidden, heads=4, concat=True, dropout=dropout)
        self.gat2 = pyg_nn.GATConv(hidden*4, hidden, heads=1, concat=True, dropout=dropout)
        self.out  = pyg_nn.Linear(hidden, num_classes)
        self.dropout = dropout

    def forward(self, x, edge_index):
        x = F.elu(self.gat1(x, edge_index))
        x = F.dropout(x, p=self.dropout, training=self.training)
        x = F.elu(self.gat2(x, edge_index))
        x = F.dropout(x, p=self.dropout, training=self.training)
        logits = self.out(x)
        return logits, x  # return hidden for surrogate rule model


# [CELL] 
def train_model(data, config=CONFIG):
    model = NeurosymbolicGNN(
        in_dim=data.x.shape[1],
        hidden=config["hidden_dim"],
        num_classes=int(data.y.max().item()+1),
        dropout=config["dropout"]
    ).to(config["device"])

    # Class weights for imbalance
    y_cpu = data.y.cpu().numpy()
    class_counts = Counter(y_cpu[data.train_mask.cpu().numpy()])
    max_c = max(class_counts.values())
    weights = torch.tensor([max_c / (class_counts.get(c, 1)) for c in range(int(data.y.max())+1)], 
                           dtype=torch.float, device=config["device"])
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(weight=weights)
    optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=config["lr"])

    best_val, best_state = -1, None
    for epoch in tqdm(range(config["epochs"]), desc="Training (Track 2)"):
        model.train()
        logits, _ = model(data.x, data.edge_index)
        loss = criterion(logits[data.train_mask], data.y[data.train_mask])

        optimizer.zero_grad()
        loss.backward()
        optimizer.step()

        # Eval on val
        model.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            val_logits, _ = model(data.x, data.edge_index)
            val_pred = val_logits.argmax(dim=1)
            val_acc = (val_pred[data.val_mask] == data.y[data.val_mask]).float().mean().item()
            if val_acc > best_val:
                best_val = val_acc
                best_state = {k: v.detach().cpu().clone() for k, v in model.state_dict().items()}

        if epoch % 25 == 0:
            logging.info(f"Epoch {epoch} | train_loss={loss.item():.4f} | val_acc={val_acc:.3f}")

    # Load best
    if best_state:
        model.load_state_dict(best_state)
    return model

model = train_model(pyg_data, CONFIG)
print("✅ Model trained.")
torch.save(model.state_dict(), os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "neurosymbolic_gnn.pt"))


# [CELL] 
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    logits, h = model(pyg_data.x, pyg_data.edge_index)
    y_pred = logits.argmax(dim=1).cpu().numpy()
    y_true = pyg_data.y.cpu().numpy()

# Split-wise metrics
def report_split(mask, name):
    m = mask.cpu().numpy()
    print(f"\n=== {name} Metrics ===")
    print(classification_report(y_true[m], y_pred[m], digits=3))
    cm = confusion_matrix(y_true[m], y_pred[m])
    plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
    sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt="d", cbar=False)
    plt.title(f"{name} Confusion Matrix")
    plt.xlabel("Predicted"); plt.ylabel("True")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

report_split(pyg_data.train_mask, "Train")
report_split(pyg_data.val_mask, "Validation")
report_split(pyg_data.test_mask, "Test")

# Save raw predictions
pred_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "neuron": X.index,
    "true": y_true,
    "pred": y_pred
})
pred_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "predictions.csv"), index=False)
print("📝 Saved predictions.csv")


# [CELL] 
# PyG's unified explainer
explainer = Explainer(
    model=model,
    algorithm=GNNExplainer(epochs=CONFIG["explainer_epochs"]),
    explanation_type="model",
    node_mask_type="attributes",
    edge_mask_type="object"
)

# Explain K nodes per class
K = 3
examples = []
for c in np.unique(y_true):
    idx_c = np.where((y_true == c) & (pyg_data.test_mask.cpu().numpy()))[0]
    idx_pick = idx_c[:K] if len(idx_c) >= K else idx_c
    for nid in idx_pick:
        examples.append((int(nid), int(c)))

attributions = []
for nid, c in tqdm(examples, desc="Explaining nodes"):
    exp = explainer(pyg_data.x, pyg_data.edge_index, index=nid)
    # Feature importances
    feat_imp = exp.node_mask[nid].detach().cpu().numpy()
    attributions.append((X.columns.tolist(), feat_imp, nid, c))

# Aggregate and save top features per class
top_k = 15
rows = []
for cols, imp, nid, c in attributions:
    order = np.argsort(imp)[::-1][:top_k]
    for rank, j in enumerate(order, 1):
        rows.append({"node_id": nid, "class": c, "feature": cols[j], "importance": float(imp[j]), "rank": rank})

attr_df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
attr_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "gnn_explanations.csv"), index=False)
print("🧠 Saved gnn_explanations.csv (top features per explained node)")

# Quick view: top global features (median across explained nodes)
agg = (attr_df.groupby("feature")["importance"].median().sort_values(ascending=False).head(25))
plt.figure(figsize=(6,6))
sns.barplot(x=agg.values, y=agg.index)
plt.title("Top median feature importances (explained nodes)")
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()


# [CELL] 
# Correlate hidden dims with original features to interpret splits
corr = pd.DataFrame(np.corrcoef(H.T, X_std.T), 
                    index=[f"h{i}" for i in range(H.shape[1])] + [f"x{i}" for i in range(X_std.shape[1])],
                    columns=[f"h{i}" for i in range(H.shape[1])] + [f"x{i}" for i in range(X_std.shape[1])])

# For each hidden feature used by the tree (first few), find top-correlated original features
used_h = set()
for line in rules_text.splitlines():
    if "h" in line and "<=" in line or ">" in line:
        token = line.strip().split()[0]
        if token.startswith("h"):
            used_h.add(token)

mapping_rows = []
for hname in sorted(used_h, key=lambda s: int(s[1:])):
    h_idx = int(hname[1:])
    # correlations with original features (the second block in corr)
    cvec = corr.loc[hname, [f"x{i}" for i in range(X_std.shape[1])]].values
    topj = np.argsort(np.abs(cvec))[::-1][:10]
    for j in topj:
        mapping_rows.append({
            "hidden": hname,
            "orig_feature": X.columns[j],
            "abs_corr": float(abs(cvec[j])),
            "signed_corr": float(cvec[j])
        })

map_df = pd.DataFrame(mapping_rows).sort_values(["hidden","abs_corr"], ascending=[True, False])
map_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, "hidden_to_feature_mapping.csv"), index=False)
print("🔗 Saved hidden_to_feature_mapping.csv (top correlations)")

map_df.head(20)


# [CELL] 


# [CELL] 
## Iter 2

# [CELL] 
import pandas as pd
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch_geometric.utils import from_networkx
from torch_geometric.loader import DataLoader
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, confusion_matrix
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from tqdm import tqdm
import numpy as np

# === Step 1: Load and align enriched features ===
motif_df = pd.read_csv("motif_summary.csv", index_col=0)
lineage_df = pd.read_pickle("lineage_embeddings.pkl")

motif_aligned = motif_df.reindex(nodes_labeled).fillna(0)
lineage_aligned = lineage_df.reindex(nodes_labeled).fillna(0)

X_combined = pd.concat([pd.DataFrame(X_std, index=nodes_labeled),
                        motif_aligned,
                        lineage_aligned], axis=1).fillna(0).values
print(f"Final enriched feature shape: {X_combined.shape}")

y_aligned = y_full.loc[nodes_labeled]

# === Step 2: Convert to PyG ===
# Strip all existing node attributes to avoid mismatch errors
G_clean = nx.Graph()
G_clean.add_nodes_from(G_sub.nodes())
G_clean.add_edges_from(G_sub.edges())

pyg_data = from_networkx(G_clean)  # no attributes pulled from NX
pyg_data.x = torch.tensor(X_combined, dtype=torch.float)
pyg_data.y = torch.tensor(y_aligned.values, dtype=torch.long)


# === Step 3: Train/Val/Test Split ===
idx = np.arange(pyg_data.num_nodes)
train_idx, test_idx = train_test_split(idx, test_size=0.2, stratify=pyg_data.y, random_state=42)
train_idx, val_idx = train_test_split(train_idx, test_size=0.2, stratify=pyg_data.y[train_idx], random_state=42)

# Masks
pyg_data.train_mask = torch.zeros(pyg_data.num_nodes, dtype=torch.bool)
pyg_data.val_mask = torch.zeros(pyg_data.num_nodes, dtype=torch.bool)
pyg_data.test_mask = torch.zeros(pyg_data.num_nodes, dtype=torch.bool)

pyg_data.train_mask[train_idx] = True
pyg_data.val_mask[val_idx] = True
pyg_data.test_mask[test_idx] = True

# === Step 4: Model ===
from torch_geometric.nn import GCNConv

class GCNClassifier(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, hidden_channels, num_classes):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = GCNConv(in_channels, hidden_channels)
        self.conv2 = GCNConv(hidden_channels, hidden_channels)
        self.lin = nn.Linear(hidden_channels, num_classes)

    def forward(self, data):
        x, edge_index = data.x, data.edge_index
        x = self.conv1(x, edge_index)
        x = F.relu(x)
        x = self.conv2(x, edge_index)
        x = F.relu(x)
        x = self.lin(x)
        return x

# === Step 5: Class weights ===
class_counts = torch.bincount(pyg_data.y[pyg_data.train_mask])
class_weights = (1.0 / class_counts.float()) * (len(pyg_data.train_mask) / len(class_counts))
class_weights = class_weights / class_weights.sum() * len(class_counts)
print(f"Class weights: {class_weights.tolist()}")

device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
model = GCNClassifier(pyg_data.num_node_features, 128, len(class_counts)).to(device)
pyg_data = pyg_data.to(device)
optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.005, weight_decay=5e-4)
criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(weight=class_weights.to(device))

# === Step 6: Training Loop ===
def train():
    model.train()
    optimizer.zero_grad()
    out = model(pyg_data)
    loss = criterion(out[pyg_data.train_mask], pyg_data.y[pyg_data.train_mask])
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()
    return loss.item()

def evaluate(mask):
    model.eval()
    with torch.no_grad():
        pred = model(pyg_data).argmax(dim=1)
        correct = pred[mask].eq(pyg_data.y[mask]).sum().item()
        total = mask.sum().item()
        acc = correct / total
    return acc, pred[mask].cpu().numpy(), pyg_data.y[mask].cpu().numpy()

EPOCHS = 1000
for epoch in tqdm(range(1, EPOCHS+1), desc="Training GCN"):
    loss = train()
    if epoch % 100 == 0 or epoch == 1:
        train_acc, _, _ = evaluate(pyg_data.train_mask)
        val_acc, _, _ = evaluate(pyg_data.val_mask)
        print(f"Epoch {epoch:03d} | Loss: {loss:.4f} | Train Acc: {train_acc:.4f} | Val Acc: {val_acc:.4f}")

# === Step 7: Final Evaluation ===
for split_name, mask in [("Train", pyg_data.train_mask), ("Validation", pyg_data.val_mask), ("Test", pyg_data.test_mask)]:
    acc, pred, true = evaluate(mask)
    print(f"\n=== {split_name} Metrics ===")
    print(classification_report(true, pred))
    cm = confusion_matrix(true, pred)
    sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt='d', cmap='mako')
    plt.title(f"{split_name} Confusion Matrix")
    plt.xlabel("Predicted")
    plt.ylabel("True")
    plt.show()


# [CELL] 
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, confusion_matrix
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import torch

# === Step 1: Safe forward call ===
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    try:
        # Try PyG-style (data object)
        logits = model(pyg_data)
    except TypeError:
        # Fallback: assume (x, edge_index) signature
        logits = model(pyg_data.x, pyg_data.edge_index)

# === Step 2: Safe extraction of test mask ===
test_mask = getattr(pyg_data, 'test_mask', None)
if test_mask is None:
    raise ValueError("❌ No test_mask found in pyg_data. Ensure dataset split was performed.")

# === Step 3: Get predictions and labels ===
y_true = pyg_data.y[test_mask]
if torch.is_tensor(y_true):
    y_true = y_true.cpu().numpy()

y_pred = logits[test_mask].argmax(dim=1)
if torch.is_tensor(y_pred):
    y_pred = y_pred.cpu().numpy()

# === Step 4: Metrics ===
print("=== Classification Report (Test) ===")
print(classification_report(y_true, y_pred, digits=4))

# Confusion matrix
cm = confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred)
plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt='d', cmap='Blues')
plt.title("Confusion Matrix (Test)")
plt.xlabel("Predicted")
plt.ylabel("True")
plt.show()

# === Step 5: Class distribution comparison ===
plt.figure(figsize=(8,4))
sns.countplot(x=y_true, color='blue', alpha=0.5, label='True')
sns.countplot(x=y_pred, color='red', alpha=0.5, label='Predicted')
plt.legend()
plt.title("Class Distribution: True vs Predicted (Test)")
plt.xlabel("Class Label")
plt.ylabel("Count")
plt.show()

# === Step 6: t-SNE Visualization ===
try:
    tsne = TSNE(n_components=2, random_state=42, perplexity=30)
    X_embedded = tsne.fit_transform(pyg_data.x[test_mask].cpu().numpy())

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12,5))
    sns.scatterplot(x=X_embedded[:,0], y=X_embedded[:,1], hue=y_true,
                    palette='Set1', ax=axes[0], legend='full', s=20)
    axes[0].set_title("t-SNE by True Label")

    sns.scatterplot(x=X_embedded[:,0], y=X_embedded[:,1], hue=y_pred,
                    palette='Set1', ax=axes[1], legend='full', s=20)
    axes[1].set_title("t-SNE by Predicted Label")

    plt.show()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"⚠️ t-SNE plot skipped due to error: {e}")

# [CELL] 
# %% [0] Imports & Environment Setup
# Purpose: Load libraries, set global paths, establish determinism, and create output dirs.

import os, sys, json, math, random, time, pathlib
from datetime import datetime
from collections import defaultdict, Counter

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# Graph & GNN
import networkx as nx
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F

# PyTorch Geometric
try:
    import torch_geometric as tg
    from torch_geometric.data import Data
    from torch_geometric.utils import from_networkx
    from torch_geometric.nn import GCNConv, GATConv
    PYG_OK = True
except Exception as e:
    print("WARNING: torch_geometric not available or misconfigured:", e)
    PYG_OK = False

# Sklearn (splits, preprocessing, metrics, calibration)
from sklearn.preprocessing import RobustScaler, StandardScaler, QuantileTransformer
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedGroupKFold
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix, roc_auc_score, precision_recall_curve, auc
from sklearn.isotonic import IsotonicRegression

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Reproducibility
def set_seed(seed: int = 42, deterministic_cudnn: bool = True):
    random.seed(seed); np.random.seed(seed)
    torch.manual_seed(seed); torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(seed)
    if deterministic_cudnn:
        torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic = True
        torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = False

set_seed(42, deterministic_cudnn=True)

# Device
DEVICE = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"[0] DEVICE={DEVICE} | torch={torch.__version__} | cuda_available={torch.cuda.is_available()}")


# [CELL]

OUT_DIR      = PROJECT_ROOT / "track2_outputs" / datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# Files you actually have
GRAPH_PATH          = DATA_DIR / "merged_master_graph.gml"  # (typo 'mastger' is correct per your file)

MOTIF_SUMMARY_PATH  = DATA_DIR / "motif_summary.csv"         # will be used for features/targets

print(f"[0] Paths:\n- GRAPH_PATH={GRAPH_PATH.exists()}\n- LINEAGE_EMB_PATH={LINEAGE_EMB_PATH.exists()}\n- MOTIF_SUMMARY_PATH={MOTIF_SUMMARY_PATH.exists()}")


# [CELL] 
# %% [1] CONFIG — Feature blocks, model choices, training, calibration, logging

CONFIG = {
    # Blocks: toggle to run ablations later
    "feature_blocks_on": {
        "A_CAUSAL": True,       # causal DAG features
        "A_INFOGEOM": True,     # information geometry / manifold coords
        "B_TOPO": True,         # loops/voids/motif counts/topology
        "C_DYNAMICS": True,     # attractor posteriors / dynamic tags
        "CORE": True            # degree/centrality/basal/NPR/region/lineage encodings
    },

    # Model backbone: "mlp" | "gcn" | "gat" | "hybrid"
    "backbone": "gcn",

    # Loss: "ce" | "focal"
    "loss": "ce",

    # Calibration: "none" | "temperature" | "isotonic"
    "calibration": "temperature",

    # Splitting policy
    "lineage_blocking": True,        # group by lineage (or region) to avoid leakage
    "group_field": "lineage_group",  # or "region_group"

    # Training
    "epochs": 200,
    "patience": 25,
    "lr": 1e-3,
    "weight_decay": 5e-4,
    "dropout": 0.25,
    "hidden_dim": 128,
    "gat_heads": 4,

    # Seeds and folds
    "seeds": [13, 21, 34],
    "n_splits": 5,

    # Logging / saving
    "save_checkpoints": True,
    "verbose": True
}

print("[1] CONFIG OK:", json.dumps(CONFIG, indent=2))


# [CELL] 
# %% [2A] Bootstrap Feature Atlas v0 from available files
# Purpose: create a minimal, high-signal feature atlas and proxy labels/groups.
import pickle
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

# 1) Create fixed output folder
FIXED_OUT_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "Track2Part2"
FIXED_OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# 2) Load graph
assert GRAPH_PATH.exists(), "Graph file missing."
G_full = nx.read_gml(GRAPH_PATH)
print(f"[2A] Loaded graph: nodes={G_full.number_of_nodes()} edges={G_full.number_of_edges()} | directed={nx.is_directed(G_full)}")

# Normalize node ids
node_ids = list(G_full.nodes())
if isinstance(node_ids[0], (int, np.integer)):
    neuron_id_list = [int(n) for n in node_ids]
else:
    neuron_id_list = [str(n) for n in node_ids]

# 3) Graph features
def safe_centrality(fn, G):
    try:
        vals = fn(G)
        return {k: float(v) for k, v in vals.items()}
    except Exception as e:
        print("Centrality fallback:", fn.__name__, e)
        return {n: 0.0 for n in G.nodes()}

deg      = dict(G_full.degree())
in_deg   = dict(G_full.in_degree()) if nx.is_directed(G_full) else {n: 0 for n in G_full.nodes()}
out_deg  = dict(G_full.out_degree()) if nx.is_directed(G_full) else {n: 0 for n in G_full.nodes()}
betw     = safe_centrality(nx.betweenness_centrality, G_full)
close    = safe_centrality(nx.closeness_centrality, G_full)
try:
    eigv = nx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(G_full)
    eigv = {k: float(v) for k, v in eigv.items()}
except Exception as e:
    print("Eigenvector fallback:", e)
    eigv = {n: 0.0 for n in G_full.nodes()}

graph_feat = pd.DataFrame({
    "neuron_id": [str(n) for n in G_full.nodes()],
    "degree": [deg[n] for n in G_full.nodes()],
    "in_degree": [in_deg[n] for n in G_full.nodes()],
    "out_degree": [out_deg[n] for n in G_full.nodes()],
    "betweenness": [betw[n] for n in G_full.nodes()],
    "closeness": [close[n] for n in G_full.nodes()],
    "eigenvector": [eigv[n] for n in G_full.nodes()],
})

# 4) Motif features
assert MOTIF_SUMMARY_PATH.exists(), "motif_summary.csv missing."
motif_df = pd.read_csv(MOTIF_SUMMARY_PATH)

# Find neuron_id column
id_candidates = [c for c in motif_df.columns if c.lower() in ["neuron_id","neuron","node","name","id"]]
if len(id_candidates) >= 1:
    motif_id_col = id_candidates[0]
elif "Unnamed: 0" in motif_df.columns:
    motif_id_col = "Unnamed: 0"
else:
    motif_df = motif_df.reset_index().rename(columns={"index": "neuron_id"})
    motif_id_col = "neuron_id"

motif_df = motif_df.rename(columns={motif_id_col: "neuron_id"})
motif_df["neuron_id"] = motif_df["neuron_id"].astype(str)

motif_num = motif_df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).copy()
motif_num["neuron_id"] = motif_df["neuron_id"]

# Merge graph + motif
atlas = graph_feat.merge(motif_num, on="neuron_id", how="left").fillna(0.0)

# 5) Lineage embeddings
with open(LINEAGE_EMB_PATH, "rb") as f:
    lin_emb = pickle.load(f)

if isinstance(lin_emb, dict):
    lin_df = pd.DataFrame({"neuron_id": list(lin_emb.keys()), "lin_vec": list(lin_emb.values())})
elif isinstance(lin_emb, pd.DataFrame):
    lin_df = lin_emb.copy()
    if "neuron_id" not in lin_df.columns:
        lin_df = lin_df.reset_index().rename(columns={"index":"neuron_id"})
else:
    raise ValueError("Unsupported lineage_embeddings format.")

lin_df["neuron_id"] = lin_df["neuron_id"].astype(str)

if "lin_vec" in lin_df.columns:
    max_len = max(len(v) for v in lin_df["lin_vec"])
    lin_mat = np.stack([np.pad(np.array(v, dtype=float), (0, max_len-len(v))) for v in lin_df["lin_vec"]], axis=0)
    lin_cols = [f"lin_{i}" for i in range(lin_mat.shape[1])]
    tmp = pd.DataFrame(lin_mat, columns=lin_cols)
    lin_df = pd.concat([lin_df[["neuron_id"]], tmp], axis=1)
else:
    lin_cols = [c for c in lin_df.columns if c != "neuron_id"]

# KMeans groups
K_GROUPS = 10
km = KMeans(n_clusters=K_GROUPS, n_init="auto", random_state=42)
lin_groups = km.fit_predict(lin_df[lin_cols].values)

groups_df = pd.DataFrame({"neuron_id": lin_df["neuron_id"], "lineage_group": lin_groups.astype(int)})
labels_proxy = pd.DataFrame({"neuron_id": lin_df["neuron_id"], "class_label": lin_groups.astype(int)})

# 6) Save artifacts in Track2Part2
FEATURE_ATLAS_PATH = FIXED_OUT_DIR / "feature_atlas_v0.parquet"
GROUPS_PATH        = FIXED_OUT_DIR / "lineage_region_groups.csv"
LABELS_PATH        = FIXED_OUT_DIR / "labels_proxy.csv"

atlas.to_parquet(FEATURE_ATLAS_PATH, index=False)
groups_df.to_csv(GROUPS_PATH, index=False)
labels_proxy.to_csv(LABELS_PATH, index=False)

print(f"[2A] Saved feature_atlas_v0: {FEATURE_ATLAS_PATH} shape={atlas.shape}")
print(f"[2A] Saved groups: {GROUPS_PATH} shape={groups_df.shape}")
print(f"[2A] Saved proxy labels: {LABELS_PATH} shape={labels_proxy.shape}")


# [CELL] 
# %% [2B] Re-derive Proxy Labels (K=3 from lineage embeddings)
# Purpose: Create a coarser, more balanced proxy target by clustering lineage embeddings into 3 groups.
# Output: Overwrites ./Track2Part2/labels_proxy.csv with the new 3-class labels.

import pickle
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

FIXED_OUT_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "Track2Part2"
LINEAGE_EMB_PATH = DATA_DIR / "lineage_embeddings.pkl"

assert FIXED_OUT_DIR.exists(), "Track2Part2 folder not found — run Cell 2A first."
assert LINEAGE_EMB_PATH.exists(), "lineage_embeddings.pkl not found."

# Load lineage embeddings (dict or DataFrame accepted)
with open(LINEAGE_EMB_PATH, "rb") as f:
    lin_emb = pickle.load(f)

if isinstance(lin_emb, dict):
    lin_df = pd.DataFrame({"neuron_id": list(lin_emb.keys()), "lin_vec": list(lin_emb.values())})
elif isinstance(lin_emb, pd.DataFrame):
    lin_df = lin_emb.copy()
    if "neuron_id" not in lin_df.columns:
        lin_df = lin_df.reset_index().rename(columns={"index": "neuron_id"})
else:
    raise ValueError("Unsupported lineage_embeddings format")

lin_df["neuron_id"] = lin_df["neuron_id"].astype(str)

# Expand vector column (if needed) into numeric columns
if "lin_vec" in lin_df.columns:
    max_len = max(len(v) for v in lin_df["lin_vec"])
    lin_mat = np.stack([np.pad(np.array(v, dtype=float), (0, max_len - len(v))) for v in lin_df["lin_vec"]], axis=0)
    lin_cols = [f"lin_{i}" for i in range(lin_mat.shape[1])]
    lin_df = pd.concat([lin_df[["neuron_id"]], pd.DataFrame(lin_mat, columns=lin_cols)], axis=1)
else:
    lin_cols = [c for c in lin_df.columns if c != "neuron_id"]

# KMeans with K=3 (coarser clusters)
K = 3
km = KMeans(n_clusters=K, n_init="auto", random_state=42)
labels_k3 = km.fit_predict(lin_df[lin_cols].values)

labels_proxy = pd.DataFrame({"neuron_id": lin_df["neuron_id"], "class_label": labels_k3.astype(int)})

# Persist (overwrites proxy labels)
LABELS_PATH = FIXED_OUT_DIR / "labels_proxy.csv"
labels_proxy.to_csv(LABELS_PATH, index=False)

# Sanity report
counts = labels_proxy["class_label"].value_counts().sort_index()
print(f"[2B] Wrote new proxy labels (K={K}) → {str(LABELS_PATH)}")
print(f"[2B] Class distribution: {dict(counts)}")
assert counts.min() > 0, "One of the K=3 classes is empty — unexpected."
print("[2B] OK. Re-run Cell [2] to reload these labels, then continue with Cell [3]+.")


# [CELL] 
# %% [2] Load Aligned Data from Track2Part2
# Purpose: Read the feature atlas, proxy labels, and lineage groups created in Cell 2A.
# These are fixed to ./Track2Part2 so you always know where to look.

import pandas as pd

# Fixed paths
FIXED_OUT_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "Track2Part2"
FEATURE_ATLAS_PATH = FIXED_OUT_DIR / "feature_atlas_v0.parquet"
LABELS_PATH        = FIXED_OUT_DIR / "labels_proxy.csv"
GROUPS_PATH        = FIXED_OUT_DIR / "lineage_region_groups.csv"

# Load
feat_df   = pd.read_parquet(FEATURE_ATLAS_PATH)
labels_df = pd.read_csv(LABELS_PATH)
groups_df = pd.read_csv(GROUPS_PATH)

# Standardize neuron_id dtype
for df in (feat_df, labels_df, groups_df):
    df["neuron_id"] = df["neuron_id"].astype(str)

# Merge labels & groups
meta_df = labels_df.merge(groups_df, on="neuron_id", how="left")
full_df = feat_df.merge(meta_df, on="neuron_id", how="inner")

# Exclude UNKNOWN class if it exists
unknown_mask = full_df["class_label"].astype(str).str.upper().eq("UNKNOWN")
unknown_ids = full_df.loc[unknown_mask, "neuron_id"].tolist()
supervised_df = full_df.loc[~unknown_mask].copy()

print(f"[2] feature_atlas_v0 shape={feat_df.shape}")
print(f"[2] labels_proxy shape={labels_df.shape}")
print(f"[2] lineage_region_groups shape={groups_df.shape}")
print(f"[2] After merge: full_df={full_df.shape} | supervised_df={supervised_df.shape} | UNKNOWN={len(unknown_ids)}")

# Sanity checks
assert "neuron_id" in supervised_df.columns, "neuron_id column missing."
assert "class_label" in supervised_df.columns, "class_label column missing."
assert "lineage_group" in supervised_df.columns, "lineage_group column missing."
assert supervised_df["neuron_id"].is_unique, "neuron_id must be unique after merge."


# [CELL] 
# %% [3] Feature Blocks & Preprocessing (with label encoding)
# Purpose: Define feature blocks, preprocess numerics/categoricals, prune collinearity,
# and encode labels into contiguous integers for PyTorch.

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import json
from sklearn.preprocessing import RobustScaler, StandardScaler, LabelEncoder

# --- 3.1 Detect feature blocks ---------------------------------

CORE_GRAPH_COLS = ["degree", "in_degree", "out_degree", "betweenness", "closeness", "eigenvector"]

present = set(supervised_df.columns)
core_cols_present = [c for c in CORE_GRAPH_COLS if c in present]
motif_cols  = [c for c in supervised_df.columns if c.startswith("motif_")]
causal_cols = [c for c in supervised_df.columns if c.startswith("causal_") or c.endswith("_causal")]
infogeom_cols = [c for c in supervised_df.columns if c.startswith("infogeom_") or c.startswith("manifold_")]
dyn_cols    = [c for c in supervised_df.columns if c.startswith("attractor_") or c.endswith("_posterior")]

BLOCKS = {
    "CORE": core_cols_present,
    "B_TOPO": motif_cols,
    "A_CAUSAL": causal_cols,
    "A_INFOGEOM": infogeom_cols,
    "C_DYNAMICS": dyn_cols,
}
BLOCKS = {k: v for k,v in BLOCKS.items() if len(v) > 0}

active_blocks = {}
for bname, cols in BLOCKS.items():
    if CONFIG["feature_blocks_on"].get(bname, False):
        active_blocks[bname] = cols

# --- 3.2 Target and grouping ----------------------------------

y_series      = supervised_df["class_label"].astype(str).copy()
groups_series = supervised_df[CONFIG["group_field"]].astype(str)

# --- 3.3 Clip tiny negative eigenvector values ----------------
if "eigenvector" in active_blocks.get("CORE", []):
    ev = supervised_df["eigenvector"].values
    supervised_df.loc[(ev < 0) & (ev > -1e-12), "eigenvector"] = 0.0

# --- 3.4 Split num/cat ----------------------------------------
cat_cols, num_cols = [], []
for block, cols in active_blocks.items():
    for c in cols:
        if supervised_df[c].dtype == "object" or supervised_df[c].dtype.name.startswith("category"):
            cat_cols.append(c)
        else:
            num_cols.append(c)
cat_cols = sorted(set(cat_cols))
num_cols = sorted(set(num_cols))

# --- 3.5 Numeric preprocessing --------------------------------
X_num = supervised_df[num_cols].copy()
if X_num.isna().sum().sum() > 0:
    X_num = X_num.fillna(X_num.median(numeric_only=True))

robust = RobustScaler()
zscaler = StandardScaler()
X_num_robust = robust.fit_transform(X_num.values)
X_num_proc   = zscaler.fit_transform(X_num_robust)

# --- 3.6 Categorical encoding --------------------------------
cat_maps, X_cat_idx = {}, None
if len(cat_cols):
    X_cat = supervised_df[cat_cols].astype(str).fillna("UNK")
    cat_arrays = []
    for c in cat_cols:
        vocab = sorted(X_cat[c].unique().tolist())
        stoi  = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(vocab)}
        cat_maps[c] = {"vocab": vocab}
        cat_arrays.append(X_cat[c].map(stoi).values.reshape(-1,1))
    X_cat_idx = np.concatenate(cat_arrays, axis=1)

# --- 3.7 Prune collinear --------------------------------------
def prune_collinear(df_num, threshold=0.98):
    if df_num.shape[1] <= 1: return np.ones(df_num.shape[1], dtype=bool)
    corr = np.corrcoef(df_num.values, rowvar=False)
    keep = np.ones(df_num.shape[1], dtype=bool)
    for i in range(df_num.shape[1]):
        if not keep[i]: continue
        for j in range(i+1, df_num.shape[1]):
            if keep[j] and abs(corr[i,j]) > threshold:
                keep[j] = False
    return keep

num_keep_mask = prune_collinear(pd.DataFrame(X_num_proc, columns=num_cols))
X_num_proc    = X_num_proc[:, num_keep_mask]
num_cols_kept = [c for c,k in zip(num_cols, num_keep_mask) if k]

# --- 3.8 Label encoding ---------------------------------------
le = LabelEncoder()
y_int = le.fit_transform(y_series.values)
classes = le.classes_.tolist()
supervised_df["class_index"] = y_int
json.dump({"classes": classes}, open(OUT_DIR / "classes.json", "w"), indent=2)

# --- 3.9 Save artifacts ---------------------------------------
np.save(OUT_DIR / "X_num_proc.npy", X_num_proc)
if X_cat_idx is not None:
    np.save(OUT_DIR / "X_cat_idx.npy", X_cat_idx)
supervised_df[["neuron_id","class_index"]].to_csv(OUT_DIR / "id_targets.csv", index=False)

feature_meta = {
    "blocks_active": list(active_blocks.keys()),
    "num_cols_kept": num_cols_kept,
    "cat_cols": cat_cols,
    "cat_maps": cat_maps,
    "classes": classes,
    "shapes": {
        "X_num_proc": list(X_num_proc.shape),
        "X_cat_idx": None if X_cat_idx is None else list(X_cat_idx.shape),
        "n_rows": int(supervised_df.shape[0])
    }
}
json.dump(feature_meta, open(OUT_DIR / "feature_meta.json", "w"), indent=2)

# --- 3.10 Sanity prints ---------------------------------------
print(f"[3] Blocks active: {list(active_blocks.keys())}")
print(f"[3] Numeric kept: {len(num_cols_kept)} | Categorical: {len(cat_cols)}")
print(f"[3] Classes: {classes}")
print(f"[3] X_num_proc shape: {X_num_proc.shape} | X_cat_idx: {None if X_cat_idx is None else X_cat_idx.shape}")


# [CELL] 
# %% [3B] Feature Builder for Ablations (isolated; mirrors Cell 3)
# Purpose: Build features for an arbitrary subset of blocks without touching OUT_DIR.
#          Saves into ABLA_DIR/<abla_name>/ and returns tensors + metadata for immediate use.

import re, json, numpy as np, pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from sklearn.preprocessing import RobustScaler, StandardScaler, LabelEncoder

def _detect_blocks(df: pd.DataFrame):
    """Auto-detect block columns (same logic as Cell 3)."""
    CORE_GRAPH_COLS = ["degree","in_degree","out_degree","betweenness","closeness","eigenvector"]
    present = set(df.columns)
    core = [c for c in CORE_GRAPH_COLS if c in present]
    topo = [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith("motif_")]
    causal = [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith("causal_") or c.endswith("_causal")]
    infog = [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith("infogeom_") or c.startswith("manifold_")]
    dyn   = [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith("attractor_") or c.endswith("_posterior")]
    blocks = {
        "CORE": core,
        "B_TOPO": topo,
        "A_CAUSAL": causal,
        "A_INFOGEOM": infog,
        "C_DYNAMICS": dyn,
    }
    return {k:v for k,v in blocks.items() if len(v) > 0}, CORE_GRAPH_COLS

def _prune_collinear(X: np.ndarray, cols, thr=0.98):
    if X.shape[1] <= 1:
        return X, cols
    corr = np.corrcoef(X, rowvar=False)
    keep = np.ones(X.shape[1], dtype=bool)
    for i in range(X.shape[1]):
        if not keep[i]: continue
        for j in range(i+1, X.shape[1]):
            if keep[j] and abs(corr[i,j]) > thr:
                keep[j] = False
    return X[:, keep], [c for c,k in zip(cols, keep) if k]

def prepare_features_for_blocks(active_block_names, abla_name: str, base_dir: Path):
    """
    Builds features for the given block subset and writes under:
        ABLA_DIR/<abla_name>/
    Returns: x_num (np.ndarray), x_cat (np.ndarray or None), y (np.ndarray[int]),
             classes (list[str]), meta (dict), save_dir (Path)
    """
    # 0) Paths
    ABLA_DIR = base_dir / "ablation_features"
    save_dir = ABLA_DIR / abla_name
    save_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # 1) Detect columns
    BLOCKS, CORE_GRAPH_COLS = _detect_blocks(supervised_df)
    available = set(BLOCKS.keys())
    wanted = [b for b in active_block_names if b in available]
    assert len(wanted) > 0, f"No requested blocks present. Requested={active_block_names}, available={sorted(available)}"

    # 2) Target & groups
    y_series = supervised_df["class_label"].astype(str).copy()
    groups_series = supervised_df[CONFIG["group_field"]].astype(str)

    # 3) Cleanup: tiny negative eigenvector
    if "CORE" in wanted and "eigenvector" in supervised_df.columns:
        ev = supervised_df["eigenvector"].values
        supervised_df.loc[(ev < 0) & (ev > -1e-12), "eigenvector"] = 0.0

    # 4) Assemble column list for this ablation
    sel_cols = []
    for b in wanted:
        sel_cols.extend(BLOCKS[b])
    # Dedup while preserving order
    seen, ordered = set(), []
    for c in sel_cols:
        if c not in seen:
            seen.add(c); ordered.append(c)
    sel_cols = ordered

    # 5) Split numeric/categorical
    cat_cols, num_cols = [], []
    for c in sel_cols:
        if supervised_df[c].dtype == "object" or str(supervised_df[c].dtype).startswith("category"):
            cat_cols.append(c)
        else:
            num_cols.append(c)

    # 6) Numeric preprocessing (robust -> z) with median impute
    X_num = supervised_df[num_cols].copy()
    if X_num.isna().sum().sum() > 0:
        X_num = X_num.fillna(X_num.median(numeric_only=True))
    robust = RobustScaler(); zscaler = StandardScaler()
    Xr = robust.fit_transform(X_num.values); Xz = zscaler.fit_transform(Xr)
    Xz, num_cols_kept = _prune_collinear(Xz, num_cols, thr=0.98)

    # 7) Categorical encoding (indices)
    X_cat, cat_maps = None, {}
    if len(cat_cols):
        Xc = supervised_df[cat_cols].astype(str).fillna("UNK")
        arrays = []
        for c in cat_cols:
            vocab = sorted(Xc[c].unique().tolist())
            stoi  = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(vocab)}
            cat_maps[c] = {"vocab": vocab}
            arrays.append(Xc[c].map(stoi).values.reshape(-1,1))
        X_cat = np.concatenate(arrays, axis=1)

    # 8) Label encoding (contiguous)
    le = LabelEncoder()
    y = le.fit_transform(y_series.values)
    classes = le.classes_.tolist()
    # keep class_index column for downstream convenience
    sf = supervised_df.copy()
    sf["class_index"] = y

    # 9) Save artifacts in ablation-specific folder
    np.save(save_dir / "X_num_proc.npy", Xz)
    if X_cat is not None:
        np.save(save_dir / "X_cat_idx.npy", X_cat)
    sf[["neuron_id","class_index"]].to_csv(save_dir / "id_targets.csv", index=False)
    meta = {
        "abla_name": abla_name,
        "blocks_requested": active_block_names,
        "blocks_used": wanted,
        "num_cols_kept": num_cols_kept,
        "cat_cols": cat_cols,
        "shapes": {
            "X_num_proc": list(Xz.shape),
            "X_cat_idx": None if X_cat is None else list(X_cat.shape),
            "n_rows": int(sf.shape[0])
        },
        "classes": classes
    }
    json.dump(meta, open(save_dir / "feature_meta.json", "w"), indent=2)

    # 10) Return tensors + meta
    return Xz, X_cat, y, classes, meta, save_dir

print("[3B] Feature builder ready: call prepare_features_for_blocks(blocks, abla_name, RUN_DIR).")


# [CELL] 
# %% [4] Graph Load & PyG Data prep (patched)
# Purpose: Load graph, align node order to supervised_df.neuron_id, create PyG data skeleton.

# Load full graph
G_full = nx.read_gml(GRAPH_PATH)

# Subgraph restricted to supervised neurons
kept_ids = set(supervised_df["neuron_id"].tolist())
G_sub = G_full.subgraph([n for n in G_full.nodes if str(n) in kept_ids]).copy()

# Ensure consistent ordering
order = supervised_df["neuron_id"].tolist()
id2idx = {nid: i for i, nid in enumerate(order)}

# Relabel to numeric indices (0..N-1)
G_ordered = nx.relabel_nodes(G_sub, id2idx, copy=True)

# 🔑 Strip all attributes so every node is uniform
for n in G_ordered.nodes:
    G_ordered.nodes[n].clear()

# Build PyG
pyg_data = from_networkx(G_ordered)

# Safety: add empty edge_index if no edges
if not hasattr(pyg_data, "edge_index"):
    pyg_data.edge_index = torch.empty((2, 0), dtype=torch.long)

N = len(order)
print(f"[4] G_sub nodes={G_sub.number_of_nodes()} edges={G_sub.number_of_edges()} | "
      f"ordered_N={N} | pyg_edges={pyg_data.edge_index.size(-1)}")
assert G_sub.number_of_nodes() == N, "Graph node count must match supervised set."


# [CELL] 
# %% [5] Split Builder (StratifiedGroupKFold with blocking)
# Purpose: Create K folds with group blocking to avoid leakage.

labels = y_series.values
groups = groups_series.values

sgkf = StratifiedGroupKFold(n_splits=CONFIG["n_splits"], shuffle=True, random_state=42)

# Build masks per split; persist to disk
SPLITS = []
for fold_idx, (tr_idx, va_idx) in enumerate(sgkf.split(np.zeros_like(labels), labels, groups)):
    mask_train = np.zeros(N, dtype=bool); mask_train[tr_idx] = True
    mask_val   = np.zeros(N, dtype=bool); mask_val[va_idx] = True

    fold_masks = {
        "train_idx": tr_idx.tolist(),
        "val_idx": va_idx.tolist()
    }
    SPLITS.append(fold_masks)
    np.savez(OUT_DIR / f"masks_fold{fold_idx}.npz", train_idx=tr_idx, val_idx=va_idx)

print(f"[5] Created {len(SPLITS)} folds with blocking on `{CONFIG['group_field']}`.")
assert len(SPLITS) == CONFIG["n_splits"], "Unexpected number of splits."


# [CELL] 
# %% [6] Tensor Assembly
# Purpose: Attach processed feature matrices and encoded labels to PyG data.

import torch

# Load numeric and optional categorical features
x_num = torch.tensor(np.load(OUT_DIR / "X_num_proc.npy"), dtype=torch.float)
x_cat = None
if (OUT_DIR / "X_cat_idx.npy").exists():
    x_cat = torch.tensor(np.load(OUT_DIR / "X_cat_idx.npy"), dtype=torch.long)

# Load encoded labels
y = torch.tensor(supervised_df["class_index"].values, dtype=torch.long)

# Attach to pyg_data
pyg_data.x_num = x_num
if x_cat is not None:
    pyg_data.x_cat = x_cat
pyg_data.y = y

print(f"[6] x_num={x_num.shape} | x_cat={None if x_cat is None else x_cat.shape} "
      f"| y={y.shape} | classes={classes}")


# [CELL] 
# %% [7] Models
# Purpose: Define baselines and GNN backbones with categorical embedding support.

class CatEmbedder(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, cat_sizes, emb_dim=16, dropout=0.0):
        super().__init__()
        self.embs = nn.ModuleList([nn.Embedding(n, min(emb_dim, max(4, int(round(np.sqrt(n)+1))))) for n in cat_sizes])
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        self.out_dim = sum(e.embedding_dim for e in self.embs)

    def forward(self, x_cat: torch.Tensor):
        if x_cat is None:
            return None
        outs = [emb(x_cat[:,i]) for i, emb in enumerate(self.embs)]
        out = torch.cat(outs, dim=-1)
        return self.drop(out)

class MLPBaseline(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=128, dropout=0.25, n_classes=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.ce = CatEmbedder(cat_sizes, emb_dim=16, dropout=dropout) if cat_sizes is not None else None
        in_dim = num_dim + (0 if self.ce is None else self.ce.out_dim)
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(in_dim, hidden),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden, hidden),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden, n_classes)
        )

    def forward(self, x_num, x_cat=None):
        if self.ce is not None and x_cat is not None:
            x = torch.cat([x_num, self.ce(x_cat)], dim=-1)
        else:
            x = x_num
        return self.net(x)

class GCNBaseline(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=128, dropout=0.25, n_classes=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.ce = CatEmbedder(cat_sizes, emb_dim=16, dropout=dropout) if cat_sizes is not None else None
        in_dim = num_dim + (0 if self.ce is None else self.ce.out_dim)
        self.lin_in = nn.Linear(in_dim, hidden)
        self.conv1 = GCNConv(hidden, hidden)
        self.conv2 = GCNConv(hidden, hidden)
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        self.out = nn.Linear(hidden, n_classes)

    def forward(self, data, x_num, x_cat=None):
        x = x_num
        if self.ce is not None and x_cat is not None:
            x = torch.cat([x_num, self.ce(x_cat)], dim=-1)
        x = self.lin_in(x)
        x = self.drop(F.relu(self.conv1(x, data.edge_index)))
        x = self.drop(F.relu(self.conv2(x, data.edge_index)))
        return self.out(x)

class GATBaseline(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=128, heads=4, dropout=0.25, n_classes=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.ce = CatEmbedder(cat_sizes, emb_dim=16, dropout=dropout) if cat_sizes is not None else None
        in_dim = num_dim + (0 if self.ce is None else self.ce.out_dim)
        self.lin_in = nn.Linear(in_dim, hidden)
        self.gat1 = GATConv(hidden, hidden // heads, heads=heads, dropout=dropout)
        self.gat2 = GATConv(hidden, hidden // heads, heads=heads, dropout=dropout)
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        self.out = nn.Linear(hidden, n_classes)

    def forward(self, data, x_num, x_cat=None):
        x = x_num
        if self.ce is not None and x_cat is not None:
            x = torch.cat([x_num, self.ce(x_cat)], dim=-1)
        x = self.lin_in(x)
        x = self.drop(F.elu(self.gat1(x, data.edge_index)))
        x = self.drop(F.elu(self.gat2(x, data.edge_index)))
        return self.out(x)

class HybridFusion(nn.Module):
    # Two streams: (A) GCN on features; (B) MLP; then fuse
    def __init__(self, num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=128, dropout=0.25, n_classes=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.gnn = GCNBaseline(num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=hidden, dropout=dropout, n_classes=hidden)
        self.mlp = MLPBaseline(num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=hidden, dropout=dropout, n_classes=hidden)
        self.fuse = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(hidden*2, hidden),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden, n_classes)
        )

    def forward(self, data, x_num, x_cat=None):
        a = self.gnn(data, x_num, x_cat)
        b = self.mlp(x_num, x_cat)
        return self.fuse(torch.cat([a, b], dim=-1))

def make_model(cfg, num_dim, cat_sizes, n_classes):
    bb = cfg["backbone"].lower()
    if bb == "mlp":
        return MLPBaseline(num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=cfg["hidden_dim"], dropout=cfg["dropout"], n_classes=n_classes)
    elif bb == "gcn":
        return GCNBaseline(num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=cfg["hidden_dim"], dropout=cfg["dropout"], n_classes=n_classes)
    elif bb == "gat":
        return GATBaseline(num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=cfg["hidden_dim"], heads=cfg["gat_heads"], dropout=cfg["dropout"], n_classes=n_classes)
    elif bb == "hybrid":
        return HybridFusion(num_dim, cat_sizes, hidden=cfg["hidden_dim"], dropout=cfg["dropout"], n_classes=n_classes)
    else:
        raise ValueError("Unknown backbone: " + bb)

# Infer dimensions from meta
feature_meta = json.load(open(OUT_DIR / "feature_meta.json"))
num_dim = len(feature_meta["num_cols_kept"])
cat_sizes = None
if len(feature_meta["cat_cols"]):
    cat_sizes = [len(feature_meta["cat_maps"][c]["vocab"]) for c in feature_meta["cat_cols"]]

n_classes = len(classes)
print(f"[7] Model dims — num_dim={num_dim} | cat_sizes={cat_sizes} | n_classes={n_classes}")
assert num_dim > 0 and n_classes >= 2, "Invalid dims."


# [CELL] 
# %% [8] Training Utils (Final CLEAN: robust metrics + no warnings + tqdm)

import json, time, warnings
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
from collections import Counter
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, balanced_accuracy_score, accuracy_score
from tqdm import trange

# Silence specific, known-benign warnings from sklearn
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", message="y_pred contains classes not in y_true")
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", message="A single label was found in 'y_true' and 'y_pred'")
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=RuntimeWarning)

CONFIG.setdefault("early_stop_metric", "balanced_acc")  # or "macro_f1"
CONFIG.setdefault("focal_gamma", 2.0)
CONFIG.setdefault("effective_beta", 0.999)
CONFIG.setdefault("zero_division", 0)

# ---------- Weights ----------
def make_weights_ce(train_counts, n_classes, device):
    total = sum(train_counts.values())
    w = torch.tensor([total / max(1, train_counts.get(i, 0)) for i in range(n_classes)],
                     dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
    return w * (n_classes / w.sum())  # normalize mean=1

def make_weights_effective_num(train_counts, n_classes, device, beta=0.999):
    weights = []
    for i in range(n_classes):
        n_i = max(1, train_counts.get(i, 0))
        eff_num = (1.0 - beta**n_i) / (1.0 - beta)
        weights.append(1.0 / eff_num)
    w = torch.tensor(weights, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
    return w * (n_classes / w.sum())

# ---------- Loss ----------
def make_loss(cfg, train_counts, n_classes, device):
    loss_type = cfg.get("loss", "ce")
    if loss_type == "focal":
        alpha = make_weights_ce(train_counts, n_classes, device)
        gamma = cfg.get("focal_gamma", 2.0)
        def focal_loss(logits, target):
            ce = F.cross_entropy(logits, target, reduction='none', weight=alpha)
            pt = torch.exp(-ce)
            return ((1 - pt) ** gamma * ce).mean()
        return focal_loss
    elif loss_type == "effective_num":
        alpha = make_weights_effective_num(train_counts, n_classes, device, beta=cfg.get("effective_beta", 0.999))
        return lambda logits, target: F.cross_entropy(logits, target, weight=alpha)
    else:
        alpha = make_weights_ce(train_counts, n_classes, device)
        return lambda logits, target: F.cross_entropy(logits, target, weight=alpha)

# ---------- Metrics (clean, robust) ----------
@torch.no_grad()
def eval_metrics_fold(logits, y_true, zero_division=0):
    """
    Robust metrics that:
     - map predictions outside 'present' labels back into present (majority) to avoid warnings,
     - handle single-class folds without crashing,
     - never create empty arrays.
    """
    y_pred = logits.argmax(dim=-1).cpu().numpy()
    y_true_np = y_true.cpu().numpy()
    present = np.unique(y_true_np)

    # Edge case: empty validation (shouldn't happen, but be safe)
    if present.size == 0 or y_true_np.size == 0:
        return {
            "macro_f1": float("nan"),
            "weighted_f1": float("nan"),
            "balanced_acc": float("nan"),
            "accuracy": float("nan"),
            "per_class_f1": {},
            "majority_balAcc": float("nan"),
        }

    # Map any predicted labels not in 'present' to the majority class of y_true
    majority_class = Counter(y_true_np).most_common(1)[0][0]
    y_pred_in_present = np.where(np.isin(y_pred, present), y_pred, majority_class)

    if present.size == 1:
        # Single-class case: compute metrics manually (no sklearn confusion matrix calls)
        c = int(present[0])
        tp = int((y_pred_in_present == c).sum())
        n  = int(y_true_np.size)
        acc = tp / n
        # For precision and recall in single-class world:
        fp = int((y_pred_in_present == c).sum() - tp)
        fn = int((y_true_np == c).sum() - tp)  # positives are all samples
        prec = tp / (tp + fp) if (tp + fp) > 0 else (0.0 if zero_division == 0 else 1.0)
        rec  = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) > 0 else (0.0 if zero_division == 0 else 1.0)
        f1 = 0.0 if (prec + rec) == 0 else (2 * prec * rec / (prec + rec))
        return {
            "macro_f1": float(f1),
            "weighted_f1": float(f1),
            "balanced_acc": float(acc),  # define as accuracy in single-class folds
            "accuracy": float(acc),
            "per_class_f1": {c: float(f1)},
            "majority_balAcc": float(1.0),  # predicting the only class gives 1.0 baseline
        }

    # Multi-class, standard metrics with labels restricted to 'present'
    macro_f1 = f1_score(y_true_np, y_pred_in_present, average="macro", labels=present, zero_division=zero_division)
    weighted_f1 = f1_score(y_true_np, y_pred_in_present, average="weighted", labels=present, zero_division=zero_division)
    bal_acc = balanced_accuracy_score(y_true_np, y_pred_in_present)
    acc = accuracy_score(y_true_np, y_pred_in_present)
    per_cls = f1_score(y_true_np, y_pred_in_present, average=None, labels=present, zero_division=zero_division)
    per_class = {int(c): float(s) for c, s in zip(present, per_cls)}

    # Majority baseline (macro recall average); for single-class handled above
    majority_preds = np.full_like(y_true_np, majority_class)
    majority_balAcc = balanced_accuracy_score(y_true_np, majority_preds)

    return {
        "macro_f1": float(macro_f1),
        "weighted_f1": float(weighted_f1),
        "balanced_acc": float(bal_acc),
        "accuracy": float(acc),
        "per_class_f1": per_class,
        "majority_balAcc": float(majority_balAcc),
    }

# ---------- Trainer ----------
def train_one_split(seed, fold_idx, cfg, pyg_data, masks, save_dir):
    set_seed(seed, deterministic_cudnn=True)

    train_idx = torch.tensor(masks["train_idx"], dtype=torch.long, device=DEVICE)
    val_idx   = torch.tensor(masks["val_idx"], dtype=torch.long, device=DEVICE)

    x_num = pyg_data.x_num.to(DEVICE)
    x_cat = getattr(pyg_data, "x_cat", None)
    if x_cat is not None:
        x_cat = x_cat.to(DEVICE)
    y     = pyg_data.y.to(DEVICE)

    feature_meta = json.load(open(OUT_DIR / "feature_meta.json"))
    num_dim = feature_meta["shapes"]["X_num_proc"][1]
    classes_json = json.load(open(OUT_DIR / "classes.json"))
    n_classes = len(classes_json["classes"])

    model = make_model(cfg, num_dim, None if x_cat is None else [x_cat.max().item()+1], n_classes).to(DEVICE)

    train_counts = Counter(y[train_idx].detach().cpu().numpy().tolist())
    criterion = make_loss(cfg, train_counts, n_classes, DEVICE)
    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=cfg["lr"], weight_decay=cfg["weight_decay"])

    metric_key = "balanced_acc" if cfg["early_stop_metric"] == "balanced_acc" else "macro_f1"
    best_score, best_state, best_epoch, no_improve = -1, None, -1, 0

    for epoch in trange(cfg["epochs"], desc=f"Seed {seed} Fold {fold_idx}", leave=False):
        model.train()
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        logits = model(pyg_data.to(DEVICE), x_num, x_cat) if cfg["backbone"] != "mlp" else model(x_num, x_cat)
        loss = criterion(logits[train_idx], y[train_idx])
        loss.backward(); optimizer.step()

        # Validation
        model.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            v_logits = model(pyg_data.to(DEVICE), x_num, x_cat) if cfg["backbone"] != "mlp" else model(x_num, x_cat)
            val_metrics = eval_metrics_fold(v_logits[val_idx], y[val_idx], zero_division=cfg["zero_division"])

        score_now = val_metrics[metric_key]
        if score_now > best_score:
            best_score = score_now
            best_state = {k: v.detach().cpu() for k, v in model.state_dict().items()}
            best_epoch = epoch
            no_improve = 0
        else:
            no_improve += 1

        if CONFIG["verbose"] and (epoch % 10 == 0 or epoch == cfg["epochs"] - 1):
            print(f"[8] seed={seed} fold={fold_idx} epoch={epoch} loss={loss.item():.4f} "
                  f"val_{metric_key}={val_metrics[metric_key]:.4f} macroF1={val_metrics['macro_f1']:.4f}")

        if no_improve >= cfg["patience"]:
            break

    if best_state is not None:
        model.load_state_dict({k: v.to(DEVICE) for k, v in best_state.items()})

    # Final train/val metrics
    model.eval()
    with torch.no_grad():
        logits = model(pyg_data.to(DEVICE), x_num, x_cat) if cfg["backbone"] != "mlp" else model(x_num, x_cat)

    tr_metrics = eval_metrics_fold(logits[train_idx], y[train_idx], zero_division=cfg["zero_division"])
    va_metrics = eval_metrics_fold(logits[val_idx], y[val_idx], zero_division=cfg["zero_division"])

    # Save
    torch.save(model.state_dict(), save_dir / f"model_seed{seed}_fold{fold_idx}.pt")
    np.save(save_dir / f"logits_seed{seed}_fold{fold_idx}.npy", logits.detach().cpu().numpy())
    json.dump({
        "seed": seed, "fold": fold_idx,
        "train": tr_metrics, "val": va_metrics,
        "best_epoch": best_epoch
    }, open(save_dir / f"metrics_seed{seed}_fold{fold_idx}.json", "w"), indent=2)

    return {"seed": seed, "fold": fold_idx, "train": tr_metrics, "val": va_metrics, "best_epoch": best_epoch}

print("[8] CLEAN training utils ready (robust metrics, no warnings, tqdm).")


# [CELL] 
print("Unique class indices:", np.unique(supervised_df["class_index"].values))
print("y.min:", supervised_df["class_index"].min(), "y.max:", supervised_df["class_index"].max())
print("n_classes expected:", len(classes))


# [CELL] 
# %% [9] Cross-validation Execution (CLEAN)
from tqdm import tqdm
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

RESULTS = []
for seed in CONFIG["seeds"]:
    for fold_idx, masks in enumerate(SPLITS):
        fold_dir = RUN_DIR / f"seed{seed}_fold{fold_idx}"
        fold_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True, parents=True)
        res = train_one_split(seed, fold_idx, CONFIG, pyg_data, masks, fold_dir)
        RESULTS.append(res)

# To DataFrame
df_res = pd.DataFrame([{
    "seed": r["seed"], "fold": r["fold"],
    "train_macroF1": r["train"]["macro_f1"],
    "val_macroF1": r["val"]["macro_f1"],
    "train_balAcc": r["train"]["balanced_acc"],
    "val_balAcc": r["val"]["balanced_acc"],
    "train_weightedF1": r["train"]["weighted_f1"],
    "val_weightedF1": r["val"]["weighted_f1"],
    "val_majority_balAcc": r["val"]["majority_balAcc"],
    "best_epoch": r["best_epoch"],
} for r in RESULTS])

# Clean summary: mean ± std across folds for each seed
summary = df_res.groupby("seed")[["val_macroF1","val_balAcc","val_weightedF1"]].agg(["mean","std"])
print("[9] CV summary (mean±std across folds):\n", summary)

df_res.to_csv(RUN_DIR / "cv_results.csv", index=False)
summary.to_csv(RUN_DIR / "cv_summary.csv")
print(f"[9] Saved: {RUN_DIR/'cv_results.csv'} and {RUN_DIR/'cv_summary.csv'}")


# [CELL] 
# %% [9B] Baseline Runner: MLP vs GCN on identical splits
# 1) Runs both backbones using the same SPLITS & config.
# 2) Saves metrics under RUN_DIR/backbone=mlp|gcn.
# 3) Prints a clean summary per seed.

import copy, json
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from tqdm import tqdm

# --- 9B.1 Choose which backbones to compare ---
BACKBONES = ["mlp", "gcn"]  # you can add "gat", "graphsage", etc. if your make_model supports them.

# --- 9B.2 Shared training config knobs for a fair comparison ---
BASE_CFG = copy.deepcopy(CONFIG)
BASE_CFG["n_splits"] = len(SPLITS)                # keep as-is
BASE_CFG["early_stop_metric"] = "balanced_acc"    # robust to imbalance
BASE_CFG["patience"] = max(40, CONFIG.get("patience", 40))
BASE_CFG["epochs"] = max(200, CONFIG.get("epochs", 200))
BASE_CFG["loss"] = BASE_CFG.get("loss", "effective_num")  # "ce"|"focal"|"effective_num"
BASE_CFG["verbose"] = False

# --- 9B.3 Where to write results ---
EXP_ROOT = RUN_DIR / "baselines_mlp_vs_gcn"
EXP_ROOT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

def run_backbone(backbone_name: str):
    cfg = copy.deepcopy(BASE_CFG)
    cfg["backbone"] = backbone_name
    run_dir = EXP_ROOT / f"backbone={backbone_name}"
    run_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True, parents=True)

    rows = []
    for seed in cfg["seeds"]:
        for fold_idx, masks in enumerate(tqdm(SPLITS, desc=f"{backbone_name} | seed {seed}", leave=False)):
            fold_dir = run_dir / f"seed{seed}_fold{fold_idx}"
            fold_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            res = train_one_split(seed, fold_idx, cfg, pyg_data, masks, fold_dir)
            rows.append({
                "backbone": backbone_name,
                "seed": res["seed"],
                "fold": res["fold"],
                "train_macroF1": res["train"]["macro_f1"],
                "val_macroF1": res["val"]["macro_f1"],
                "train_balAcc": res["train"]["balanced_acc"],
                "val_balAcc": res["val"]["balanced_acc"],
                "train_weightedF1": res["train"]["weighted_f1"],
                "val_weightedF1": res["val"]["weighted_f1"],
                "val_majority_balAcc": res["val"]["majority_balAcc"],
                "best_epoch": res["best_epoch"],
            })
    df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
    df.to_csv(run_dir / "cv_results.csv", index=False)
    summ = df.groupby("seed")[["val_macroF1","val_balAcc","val_weightedF1"]].agg(["mean","std"])
    summ.to_csv(run_dir / "cv_summary.csv")
    print(f"[9B] {backbone_name} summary (mean±std across folds):\n{summ}\n")
    return df

# --- 9B.4 Execute both backbones and keep a combined table ---
all_results = []
for bb in BACKBONES:
    df_bb = run_backbone(bb)
    all_results.append(df_bb)

DF_ALL = pd.concat(all_results, ignore_index=True)
DF_ALL.to_csv(EXP_ROOT / "cv_results_all.csv", index=False)

# --- 9B.5 Quick side-by-side pivot (mean across folds per seed) ---
pivot = (DF_ALL
         .groupby(["backbone","seed"])[["val_macroF1","val_balAcc","val_weightedF1"]]
         .mean()
         .reset_index())
print("[9B] Side-by-side means per seed:")
print(pivot.pivot(index="seed", columns="backbone", values="val_macroF1"))
print(pivot.pivot(index="seed", columns="backbone", values="val_balAcc"))

# --- 9B.6 Sanity: ensure both backbones produced the same number of fold results ---
for bb in BACKBONES:
    assert (DF_ALL["backbone"] == bb).sum() == len(CONFIG["seeds"]) * len(SPLITS), f"Missing results for {bb}"

print(f"[9B] Saved all runs under: {EXP_ROOT}")


# [CELL] 
# %% [10B] Statistical Comparison (GCN − MLP) + Plots
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path
from scipy import stats
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

EXP_ROOT = RUN_DIR / "baselines_mlp_vs_gcn"
DF_ALL = pd.read_csv(EXP_ROOT / "cv_results_all.csv")

# --- 10B.1 Align rows by (seed, fold) for fair paired deltas ---
def metric_table(df, metric):
    return (df.pivot_table(index=["seed","fold"], columns="backbone", values=metric)
              .dropna(how="any")
              .sort_index())

metrics = ["val_macroF1", "val_balAcc", "val_weightedF1"]
tables = {m: metric_table(DF_ALL, m) for m in metrics}

# --- 10B.2 Compute paired deltas and t-tests ---
results = []
for m, T in tables.items():
    if not {"mlp","gcn"}.issubset(T.columns):
        print(f"[10B] Skipping {m} (one backbone missing).")
        continue
    delta = T["gcn"] - T["mlp"]
    mu, sd = float(delta.mean()), float(delta.std(ddof=1))
    t, p = stats.ttest_rel(T["gcn"], T["mlp"])
    results.append({"metric": m, "mean_delta": mu, "std_delta": sd, "t_stat": float(t), "p_value": float(p), "n": int(len(delta))})

    # Save per-pair deltas
    out_pairs = EXP_ROOT / f"paired_deltas_{m}.csv"
    delta.reset_index().rename(columns={0:"delta"}).to_csv(out_pairs, index=False)

DF_STATS = pd.DataFrame(results)
DF_STATS.to_csv(EXP_ROOT / "paired_stats.csv", index=False)

print("[10B] Paired GCN−MLP deltas (seed,fold-aligned):")
print(DF_STATS)

# --- 10B.3 Quick bar plots (optional) ---
# (Each chart saved; plots do not specify styles or colors per your environment rules)
for m, T in tables.items():
    if not {"mlp","gcn"}.issubset(T.columns):
        continue
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6,4))
    means = [T["mlp"].mean(), T["gcn"].mean()]
    xs = np.arange(2)
    ax.bar(xs, means)  # default colors
    ax.set_xticks(xs); ax.set_xticklabels(["MLP","GCN"])
    ax.set_ylabel(m)
    ax.set_title(f"{m}: mean across all (seed,fold)")
    fig.tight_layout()
    fig_path = EXP_ROOT / f"plot_{m}_mean_bar.png"
    plt.savefig(fig_path, dpi=150)
    plt.close(fig)

print(f"[10B] Saved stats & plots to: {EXP_ROOT}")


# [CELL] 
# %% [9C] Ablation Runner (auto-discovers blocks, builds features per set, runs CV cleanly)

import json, copy
import numpy as np, pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from tqdm import tqdm

# --- 9C.1 Discover available blocks in current atlas --------------------------
def discover_available_blocks():
    blk = {}
    blk["CORE"]       = any(c in supervised_df.columns for c in ["degree","in_degree","out_degree","betweenness","closeness","eigenvector"])
    blk["B_TOPO"]     = any(c.startswith("motif_") for c in supervised_df.columns)
    blk["A_CAUSAL"]   = any(c.startswith("causal_") or c.endswith("_causal") for c in supervised_df.columns)
    blk["A_INFOGEOM"] = any(c.startswith("infogeom_") or c.startswith("manifold_") for c in supervised_df.columns)
    blk["C_DYNAMICS"] = any(c.startswith("attractor_") or c.endswith("_posterior") for c in supervised_df.columns)
    return [b for b, ok in blk.items() if ok]

AVAILABLE_BLOCKS = discover_available_blocks()
print(f"[9C] Available blocks: {AVAILABLE_BLOCKS}")

# --- 9C.2 Define the ablation sets you want to run ----------------------------
# Compose sensible subsets from what's actually available.
ABLA_SETS = []

if "CORE" in AVAILABLE_BLOCKS:
    ABLA_SETS.append(("core_only", ["CORE"]))
if "B_TOPO" in AVAILABLE_BLOCKS:
    ABLA_SETS.append(("topo_only", ["B_TOPO"]))
if {"CORE","B_TOPO"}.issubset(AVAILABLE_BLOCKS):
    ABLA_SETS.append(("core+topo", ["CORE","B_TOPO"]))

# Add "ALL" = every available block
ABLA_SETS.append(("all_blocks", AVAILABLE_BLOCKS.copy()))

print("[9C] Planned ablations:")
for name, blocks in ABLA_SETS:
    print(f"  - {name}: {blocks}")

# --- 9C.3 Helper: run CV using features from an ablation folder ---------------
def run_cv_with_features_from(save_dir: Path, cfg_base):
    """
    Loads X_num/X_cat/y from save_dir (produced by prepare_features_for_blocks),
    attaches them to pyg_data for this run only, and invokes train_one_split.
    Returns a DataFrame of fold-level results.
    """
    # Load features
    X_num = np.load(save_dir / "X_num_proc.npy")
    X_cat = np.load(save_dir / "X_cat_idx.npy") if (save_dir / "X_cat_idx.npy").exists() else None
    meta  = json.load(open(save_dir / "feature_meta.json"))
    classes = meta["classes"]; n_classes = len(classes)

    # Attach to pyg_data (temporary)
    x_num_t = torch.tensor(X_num, dtype=torch.float, device=DEVICE)
    x_cat_t = torch.tensor(X_cat, dtype=torch.long, device=DEVICE) if X_cat is not None else None
    y_vec   = pd.read_csv(save_dir / "id_targets.csv")["class_index"].values
    y_t     = torch.tensor(y_vec, dtype=torch.long, device=DEVICE)

    # Save original references to restore after run
    x_num_backup = getattr(pyg_data, "x_num", None)
    x_cat_backup = getattr(pyg_data, "x_cat", None)
    y_backup     = getattr(pyg_data, "y", None)

    pyg_data.x_num = x_num_t
    if x_cat_t is not None: pyg_data.x_cat = x_cat_t
    elif hasattr(pyg_data, "x_cat"): delattr(pyg_data, "x_cat")
    pyg_data.y = y_t

    # Config (do not change global CONFIG except backbone/epochs etc)
    cfg = copy.deepcopy(CONFIG)
    # IMPORTANT: ensure model dims and classes are consistent with this ablation
    # (train_one_split reads num_dim & classes from feature_meta/classes.json in OUT_DIR;
    #  so we temporarily write ablation meta to OUT_DIR to keep code simple)
    # We'll stash, then restore.
    OUT_BAK_META = None
    OUT_BAK_CLASSES = None
    if (OUT_DIR / "feature_meta.json").exists():
        OUT_BAK_META = json.load(open(OUT_DIR / "feature_meta.json"))
    if (OUT_DIR / "classes.json").exists():
        OUT_BAK_CLASSES = json.load(open(OUT_DIR / "classes.json"))

    json.dump(meta, open(OUT_DIR / "feature_meta.json", "w"), indent=2)
    json.dump({"classes": classes}, open(OUT_DIR / "classes.json", "w"), indent=2)

    rows = []
    abla_run_dir = RUN_DIR / f"ablation_runs/{save_dir.name}"
    abla_run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    for seed in cfg["seeds"]:
        for fold_idx, masks in enumerate(tqdm(SPLITS, desc=f"{save_dir.name} | seed {seed}", leave=False)):
            fold_dir = abla_run_dir / f"seed{seed}_fold{fold_idx}"
            fold_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            res = train_one_split(seed, fold_idx, cfg, pyg_data, masks, fold_dir)
            rows.append({
                "abla": save_dir.name,
                "seed": res["seed"], "fold": res["fold"],
                "train_macroF1": res["train"]["macro_f1"], "val_macroF1": res["val"]["macro_f1"],
                "train_balAcc": res["train"]["balanced_acc"], "val_balAcc": res["val"]["balanced_acc"],
                "train_weightedF1": res["train"]["weighted_f1"], "val_weightedF1": res["val"]["weighted_f1"],
                "best_epoch": res["best_epoch"]
            })

    # Restore OUT_DIR meta files and pyg_data tensors
    if OUT_BAK_META is not None:
        json.dump(OUT_BAK_META, open(OUT_DIR / "feature_meta.json", "w"), indent=2)
    if OUT_BAK_CLASSES is not None:
        json.dump(OUT_BAK_CLASSES, open(OUT_DIR / "classes.json", "w"), indent=2)
    pyg_data.x_num = x_num_backup
    if x_cat_backup is not None: pyg_data.x_cat = x_cat_backup
    elif hasattr(pyg_data, "x_cat"): delattr(pyg_data, "x_cat")
    pyg_data.y = y_backup

    df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
    df.to_csv(abla_run_dir / "cv_results.csv", index=False)
    summ = df.groupby("seed")[["val_macroF1","val_balAcc","val_weightedF1"]].agg(["mean","std"])
    summ.to_csv(abla_run_dir / "cv_summary.csv")
    return df, summ, abla_run_dir

# --- 9C.4 Execute: Build features per ablation set, then run CV ----------------
ALL_ABLA_RESULTS = []
BASE_DIR = RUN_DIR  # keep ablation artifacts under the current run folder

for abla_name, blocks in ABLA_SETS:
    # Build features for this block subset (into ABLA_DIR/<abla_name>)
    Xz, Xc, yv, classes, meta, save_dir = prepare_features_for_blocks(blocks, abla_name, BASE_DIR)
    print(f"[9C] Built features for '{abla_name}': X_num={Xz.shape}, X_cat={None if Xc is None else Xc.shape}, classes={len(classes)}")

    # Run CV and collect metrics
    df_res, df_summ, arun_dir = run_cv_with_features_from(save_dir, CONFIG)
    print(f"[9C] {abla_name} summary (mean±std across folds):\n{df_summ}\n")
    ALL_ABLA_RESULTS.append(df_res)

# --- 9C.5 Combined comparison table -------------------------------------------
ABL_ALL = pd.concat(ALL_ABLA_RESULTS, ignore_index=True)
ABL_PIV = (ABL_ALL
           .groupby(["abla","seed"])[["val_macroF1","val_balAcc","val_weightedF1"]]
           .mean()
           .reset_index())
print("[9C] Side-by-side (per-seed means):")
print(ABL_PIV.pivot(index="seed", columns="abla", values="val_macroF1"))

# Save combined
ABL_ROOT = RUN_DIR / "ablation_runs"
ABL_ALL.to_csv(ABL_ROOT / "ablation_cv_results_all.csv", index=False)
ABL_PIV.to_csv(ABL_ROOT / "ablation_cv_seed_means.csv", index=False)
print(f"[9C] Saved all ablation runs under: {ABL_ROOT}")

# [CELL] 
# === Track 3 — Part 1 (Data Freeze) & Part 2 (Splits) ===
# Notebook: track3_part1_part2.ipynb
# Requirements: pandas, numpy, networkx, scipy, scikit-learn, tqdm, pyarrow, fastparquet

import os, json, math, sys, re, warnings
from pathlib import Path
from collections import defaultdict, Counter

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import networkx as nx

from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedShuffleSplit
from sklearn.metrics import pairwise_distances
from tqdm import tqdm

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning)

# --- Project paths
ROOT = Path.cwd()
T3 = ROOT / "track3"
DATA = T3 / "data"
SPLITS = DATA / "splits"
REPORTS = T3 / "reports"
FIGS = REPORTS / "figures"

for p in [DATA, SPLITS, REPORTS, FIGS]:
    p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# --- Input files (adjust if your filenames differ)
FILE_MOTIFS   = ROOT / "motif_participation_profiles.csv"   # expects 'Neuron' + 419 motif columns
FILE_LATENTS  = ROOT / "latents_k2.pkl"                     # DataFrame or dict with index 'Neuron' (110 dims)
FILE_META     = ROOT / "neuron_metadata.csv"                # expects Neuron, Type, optional: BodyRegion, Side, IsGlia

# --- Split outputs
SEEDS = [13, 21, 34, 55, 89]
SPLIT_FILES_RANDOM = {s: SPLITS / f"random_seed{s}.json" for s in SEEDS}

GROUPS_FILES       = {s: SPLITS / f"lineage_groups_seed{s}.parquet" for s in SEEDS}
AUDIT_DIR          = REPORTS / "split_audits"
AUDIT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

print("✔️ Paths ready.")


# [CELL] 
def read_latents_any(path: Path, fallback_index: pd.Index | None = None) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Robust loader for lineage/latent embeddings → DataFrame with index 'Neuron' and columns lin_0..lin_(d-1).

    Now supports: list of 2D tensors/arrays where each item corresponds to ONE neuron/graph
    and contains rows for nodes within that graph. We take the FIRST ROW per item (center neuron),
    matching our Track 1 convention.
    """
    obj = pd.read_pickle(path)

    def to_numpy(x):
        # Duck-typed torch.Tensor support without importing torch
        if hasattr(x, "detach") and callable(getattr(x, "detach")):
            try:
                return x.detach().cpu().numpy()
            except Exception:
                return np.asarray(x)
        return np.asarray(x)

    def to_df(ids, X):
        X = to_numpy(X)
        if X.ndim == 1:
            X = X.reshape(-1, 1)
        if ids is None:
            if fallback_index is None or len(fallback_index) != X.shape[0]:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"No neuron IDs provided and fallback_index is unusable. "
                    f"Got X shape {X.shape}, fallback_index len "
                    f"{len(fallback_index) if fallback_index is not None else 'None'}."
                )
            ids = list(fallback_index)
        cols = [f"lin_{i}" for i in range(X.shape[1])]
        return pd.DataFrame(X, index=pd.Index(ids, name="Neuron"), columns=cols)

    # --- Direct formats handled early ---
    if isinstance(obj, pd.DataFrame):
        df = obj.copy()
        if 'Neuron' in df.columns:
            df = df.set_index('Neuron')
        new_cols = {c: f"lin_{i}" for i, c in enumerate(df.columns) if not str(c).startswith("lin_")}
        return df.rename(columns=new_cols)

    if isinstance(obj, dict):
        low = {k.lower(): k for k in obj.keys()}
        id_keys = [k for k in ['neurons','ids','index','nodes','names'] if k in low]
        x_keys  = [k for k in ['x','z','emb','embeddings','latents','vectors','data','array'] if k in low]

        ids = None
        X = None
        if x_keys:
            X = obj[low[x_keys[0]]]
        if id_keys:
            ids = obj[low[id_keys[0]]]
            if isinstance(ids, (pd.Index, pd.Series)):
                ids = list(ids.values if isinstance(ids, pd.Series) else ids)

        if isinstance(X, pd.DataFrame):
            df = X.copy()
            if 'Neuron' in df.columns:
                df = df.set_index('Neuron')
            new_cols = {c: f"lin_{i}" for i, c in enumerate(df.columns) if not str(c).startswith("lin_")}
            return df.rename(columns=new_cols)

        if X is not None:
            return to_df(ids, X)

        raise ValueError("Unsupported dict structure for latents. Keys: " + ", ".join(obj.keys()))

    if isinstance(obj, (tuple, list)) and len(obj) == 2 and not isinstance(obj[0], dict):
        a, b = obj
        if isinstance(a, (list, tuple, pd.Index, pd.Series)) and (hasattr(b, "shape") or hasattr(b, "detach")):
            ids = list(a if not isinstance(a, pd.Series) else a.values)
            return to_df(ids, b)
        if (hasattr(a, "shape") or hasattr(a, "detach")) and isinstance(b, (list, tuple, pd.Index, pd.Series)):
            ids = list(b if not isinstance(b, pd.Series) else b.values)
            return to_df(ids, a)
        if hasattr(a, "shape") or hasattr(a, "detach"):
            return to_df(None, a)

    # --- LIST formats (including list of tensors/arrays) ---
    if isinstance(obj, list):
        if len(obj) == 0:
            raise ValueError("Latents list is empty.")

        first = obj[0]

        # Single wrapper → unwrap
        if len(obj) == 1 and (isinstance(first, (pd.DataFrame, np.ndarray, list)) or hasattr(first, "detach")):
            inner = first
            if isinstance(inner, pd.DataFrame):
                df = inner.copy()
                if 'Neuron' in df.columns:
                    df = df.set_index('Neuron')
                new_cols = {c: f"lin_{i}" for i, c in enumerate(df.columns) if not str(c).startswith("lin_")}
                return df.rename(columns=new_cols)
            return to_df(None, inner)

        # List of dicts
        if isinstance(first, dict):
            id_keys = ['neuron','id','name','node','Neuron','ID','Name']
            x_keys  = ['embedding','emb','vector','latent','feat','features','X','data']
            ids, Xs = [], []
            for item in obj:
                nid = next((item[k] for k in id_keys if k in item), None)
                vec_raw = next((item[k] for k in x_keys if k in item), None)
                if vec_raw is None:
                    raise ValueError("Latents dict item missing embedding/vector.")
                vec = to_numpy(vec_raw)
                # If 2D, take first row (center neuron)
                if vec.ndim == 2:
                    vec = vec[0]
                Xs.append(vec)
                ids.append(nid)
            X = np.vstack([x.reshape(1, -1) if x.ndim == 1 else x for x in Xs])
            if any(i is None for i in ids):
